逐瘀法系Blood-Stasis Lineage

类方鉴别 · 类别枢纽Formula Class Hub
本类方剂（7）
桃红四物汤Tao Hong Si Wu Tang
Tao Hong Si Wu Tang
血虚兼瘀之月经不调·经行量少有块、色紫暗
血府逐瘀汤Xue Fu Zhu Yu Tang
Xue Fu Zhu Yu Tang
胸痛日久、痛有定处如针刺·头痛经年不愈
身痛逐瘀汤Shen Tong Zhu Yu Tang
Shen Tong Zhu Yu Tang
肩痛臂痛腰痛腿痛周身疼痛·经久不愈、痛有定处
补阳还五汤Bu Yang Huan Wu Tang
Bu Yang Huan Wu Tang
中风后半身不遂、口眼㖞斜·语言謇涩、口角流涎
失笑散Shi Xiao San
Shi Xiao San
瘀血停滞之心腹刺痛·产后恶露不行、少腹急痛
丹参饮Dan Shen Yin
Dan Shen Yin
心胃气血瘀滞之心痛胃脘痛·痛处固定、时作时止
膈下逐瘀汤Ge Xia Zhu Yu Tang
Ge Xia Zhu Yu Tang
膈下（胁腹）积块、痛有定处·卧则腹坠、久泻不愈（气滞血瘀型）
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：先认瘀（久痛·刺痛·定处·夜甚·舌暗脉涩五指纹），再问三事——瘀在哪（胸中血府：血府逐瘀／肢体经络：身痛逐瘀／心胃：丹参饮／胞宫：桃红四物）、气之虚实（气滞并重：血府／气虚为本：补阳还五）、要多大火力（最小对：失笑散／底盘：桃红四物／重装：诸逐瘀）
逐瘀法系两端接经方：上端接桃核承气／抵当／桂枝茯苓丸（仲景蓄血癥瘕之攻）、下端接胶艾汤（四物之母）。王清任是本系的枢纽人物：以“气血”为纲重写瘀血学——气滞血瘀者血府逐瘀（合四逆散）、气虚血瘀者补阳还五（芪四两独尊）——同一个瘀字，气之虚实定两条相反的路。瘀血五指纹（久痛刺痛定处夜甚舌暗脉涩）是全系通行证，“怪病从瘀论治”是它的扩展条款。与经方攻瘀诸方的分工：仲景之瘀多急多实（蓄血发狂、癥痼害胎），后世之瘀多缓多杂（久痛、中风后、怪症）——急则峻攻、缓则通养。
法系演化树
胶艾汤（经方 · 妇人血家底盘）──去胶艾→ 四物汤 ──＋桃仁红花→ 桃红四物汤（养行七三）
桃红四物 ──合四逆散＋桔梗牛膝（一升一降）→ 血府逐瘀汤（气滞血瘀之枢）
桃红半壁 ──＋灵脂没药（止痛队）＋秦艽羌活（风药之末）＋地龙→ 身痛逐瘀汤（久痹入络）
血府逐瘀 ──同底盘换腹部行气药（乌药香附延胡）→ 膈下逐瘀汤（胁腹积块）
黄芪桂枝五物汤（经方 · 芪领血行）──芪升至四两＋桃红归芎地龙→ 补阳还五汤（气虚血瘀之中风）
最小单元：蒲黄＋五灵脂→ 失笑散（瘀痛小对，对读经方枳实芍药散之气分小对）
心胃轻剂：丹参一两＋檀砂→ 丹参饮（与栝楼薤白分掌胸痛之瘀与痹）
※ 峻攻端参经方：桃核承气 · 抵当汤 · 桂枝茯苓丸（缓消）
临证决策链（抓手 → 选方 → 推理）
经少色暗有块、痛经刺痛（血虚兼瘀）
→
桃红四物汤
养行七三之底盘——量多无瘀者禁。
胸痛头痛日久、刺痛定处、怪症瞀闷、舌暗脉涩
→
血府逐瘀汤
气血同治之枢：五指纹＋怪病从瘀。
周身肢体痛经久不愈、夜甚定处（痹久）
→
身痛逐瘀汤
久痹入络从瘀治——与蠲痹（邪盛）独活寄生（虚甚）按病程三分。
中风后半身不遂、脉缓无力
→
补阳还五汤
气虚血瘀：芪120g独尊——与镇肝熄风之实证中风是生死之辨（脉力定夺）。
心腹刺痛、产后恶露不行之急痛
→
失笑散
瘀痛最小对：蒲黄兼止血，痛而兼漏不忌。
心胃刺痛兼闷胀、时作时止
→
丹参饮
七血三气之轻剂——苔腻痰浊之胸痹归蒌薤。
胁腹积块、痛处不移、卧则腹坠（膈下之瘀）
→
膈下逐瘀汤
五逐瘀之腹部分册：桃红底盘换腹部行气加味（乌药香附延胡）——部位定佐使。
常见误用
第一陷阱是无瘀滥攻：胀痛走窜属气、隐痛喜按属虚——五指纹不齐勿动桃红。第二陷阱是中风两型之混（本卡与和肝卡共设）：补阳还五误用于阳亢实证等于火上浇油，脉之有力无力是一票裁判。第三陷阱是只攻不养：瘀去新血须生，久用破血必伤正——桃红四物之“四物底”与补阳还五之“芪为君”都在示范攻中有养。孕妇与出血倾向者全系慎忌。
Formulas in This Class (7)
桃红四物汤Tao Hong Si Wu Tang
Tao Hong Si Wu Tang
血虚兼瘀之月经不调·经行量少有块、色紫暗
血府逐瘀汤Xue Fu Zhu Yu Tang
Xue Fu Zhu Yu Tang
胸痛日久、痛有定处如针刺·头痛经年不愈
身痛逐瘀汤Shen Tong Zhu Yu Tang
Shen Tong Zhu Yu Tang
肩痛臂痛腰痛腿痛周身疼痛·经久不愈、痛有定处
补阳还五汤Bu Yang Huan Wu Tang
Bu Yang Huan Wu Tang
中风后半身不遂、口眼㖞斜·语言謇涩、口角流涎
失笑散Shi Xiao San
Shi Xiao San
瘀血停滞之心腹刺痛·产后恶露不行、少腹急痛
丹参饮Dan Shen Yin
Dan Shen Yin
心胃气血瘀滞之心痛胃脘痛·痛处固定、时作时止
膈下逐瘀汤Ge Xia Zhu Yu Tang
Ge Xia Zhu Yu Tang
膈下（胁腹）积块、痛有定处·卧则腹坠、久泻不愈（气滞血瘀型）
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: first recognize stasis (the five fingerprints — chronic pain, stabbing pain, fixed location, worse at night, dark tongue and choppy pulse), then ask three things — where is the stasis (blood-mansion of the chest: Xue Fu Zhu Yu / limbs and collaterals: Shen Tong Zhu Yu / Heart-stomach: Dan Shen Yin / womb: Tao Hong Si Wu), qi deficiency or repletion (qi stagnation equal-weighted: Xue Fu / qi deficiency at root: Bu Yang Huan Wu), how much firepower (smallest pair: Shi Xiao San / base: Tao Hong Si Wu / heavy armor: the various Zhu Yu formulas)
The stasis-expelling lineage joins classical formulas at both ends: the upper end to Tao He Cheng Qi / Di Dang / Gui Zhi Fu Ling Wan (Zhang Ji's attack on amassed blood and concretions), the lower end to Jiao Ai Tang (the mother of Si Wu). Wang Qingren is the lineage's pivotal figure: rewriting blood-stasis studies with "qi-blood" as the guiding thread — qi-stagnation blood-stasis takes Xue Fu Zhu Yu (combining Si Ni San), qi-deficiency blood-stasis takes Bu Yang Huan Wu (four liang of Huang Qi alone honored) — one word "stasis," and the deficiency or repletion of qi sets two opposite roads. The five stasis fingerprints (chronic pain, stabbing pain, fixed location, worse at night, dark tongue and choppy pulse) are the passport of the whole lineage, and "treat strange diseases from stasis" is its extension clause. The division with the classical stasis-attacking formulas: Zhang Ji's stasis is mostly acute and replete (amassed blood with mania, entrenched concretions harming the fetus), the later stasis is mostly gradual and mixed (chronic pain, post-stroke, strange symptoms) — the acute is fiercely attacked, the gradual is freed and nourished.
Lineage Evolution
Jiao Ai Tang (classical · the gynecological blood-family base) ── drop E Jiao-Ai Ye → Si Wu Tang ── + Tao Ren-Hong Hua → Tao Hong Si Wu Tang (nourish-move seven to three)
Tao Hong Si Wu ── combine Si Ni San + Jie Geng-Niu Xi (one ascending, one descending) → Xue Fu Zhu Yu Tang (the pivot of qi-stagnation blood-stasis)
Half of Tao-Hong ── + Wu Ling Zhi-Mo Yao (the pain-stopping team) + Qin Jiao-Qiang Huo (the tail of wind herbs) + Di Long → Shen Tong Zhu Yu Tang (chronic bi entering the collaterals)
Xue Fu Zhu Yu ── same base, swap in abdominal qi-movers (Wu Yao-Xiang Fu-Yan Hu) → Ge Xia Zhu Yu Tang (hypochondriac-abdominal masses)
Huang Qi Gui Zhi Wu Wu Tang (classical · Huang Qi leading blood to move) ── Huang Qi raised to four liang + Tao-Hong-Dang Gui-Chuan Xiong-Di Long → Bu Yang Huan Wu Tang (qi-deficiency blood-stasis stroke)
The smallest unit: Pu Huang + Wu Ling Zhi → Shi Xiao San (the small stasis-pain pair, read against the classical Zhi Shi Shao Yao San qi-level small pair)
The Heart-stomach light formula: one liang of Dan Shen + Tan-Sha → Dan Shen Yin (dividing chest pain's stasis and bi with Gua Lou Xie Bai)
※ The fierce-attack end refers to classical formulas: Tao He Cheng Qi · Di Dang Tang · Gui Zhi Fu Ling Wan (gentle dissolving)
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Scant dark menses with clots, stabbing dysmenorrhea (blood-deficiency with stasis)
→
Tao Hong Si Wu Tang
The nourish-move seven-to-three base — forbidden where menses are heavy without stasis.
Chronic chest pain and headache, stabbing pain in a fixed place, strange oppressive symptoms, dark tongue, choppy pulse
→
Xue Fu Zhu Yu Tang
The pivot of treating qi and blood together: the five fingerprints + treating strange diseases from stasis.
Whole-body limb pain long unhealed, worse at night in a fixed place (chronic bi)
→
Shen Tong Zhu Yu Tang
Chronic bi entering the collaterals treated from stasis — a three-way division of the disease course with Juan Bi (exuberant pathogen) and Du Huo Ji Sheng (severe deficiency).
Post-stroke hemiplegia, moderate forceless pulse
→
Bu Yang Huan Wu Tang
Qi-deficiency blood-stasis: 120g of Huang Qi alone honored — a life-death distinction from Zhen Gan Xi Feng's repletion stroke (the pulse strength decides).
Stabbing heart-abdomen pain, acute pain of retained postpartum lochia
→
Shi Xiao San
The smallest stasis-pain pair: Pu Huang also stops bleeding, not shunned in pain with leakage.
Stabbing heart-stomach pain with oppression, intermittent
→
Dan Shen Yin
The light formula of seven blood, three qi — chest bi of greasy coat and phlegm-turbidity goes to Gua Lou Xie Bai.
Hypochondriac-abdominal masses, pain not shifting, abdomen dragging when lying (stasis below the diaphragm)
→
Ge Xia Zhu Yu Tang
The abdominal volume of the five Zhu Yu: the Tao-Hong base with abdominal qi-movers added (Wu Yao-Xiang Fu-Yan Hu) — the location sets the assistants.
Common Misuse
The first trap is attacking without stasis: wandering distending pain is qi, faint pain relieved by pressure is deficiency — do not touch Tao-Hong when the five fingerprints are incomplete. The second trap is confusing the two stroke types (set jointly with the Liver-harmonizing card): Bu Yang Huan Wu misused for a yang-hyperactive repletion pattern is pouring oil on fire, and the pulse's strength is the sole judge. The third trap is attacking without nourishing: stasis gone, new blood must be engendered, and long use of blood-breaking must harm the upright — Tao Hong Si Wu's "Si Wu base" and Bu Yang Huan Wu's "Huang Qi as sovereign" both demonstrate nourishing within attacking. The whole lineage is used with caution in pregnancy and bleeding tendency.

温病法系Wenbing Lineage

类方鉴别 · 类别枢纽Formula Class Hub
本类方剂（7）
桑菊饮Sang Ju Yin
Sang Ju Yin
但咳、身热不甚·口微渴
银翘散Yin Qiao San
Yin Qiao San
发热无汗或有汗不畅·微恶风寒、头痛
沙参麦冬汤Sha Shen Mai Dong Tang
Sha Shen Mai Dong Tang
燥伤肺胃阴分·咽干口渴
益胃汤Yi Wei Tang
Yi Wei Tang
热病后胃阴受伤·不饥不食（饥不欲食）
增液汤Zeng Ye Tang
Zeng Ye Tang
阳明温病、津亏便秘·大便干结、数日不行而无痞满燥实之全局
养阴清肺汤Yang Yin Qing Fei Tang
Yang Yin Qing Fei Tang
阴虚燥热之咽喉肿痛（白喉及虚火喉痹）·咽干鼻燥
杏苏散Xing Su San
Xing Su San
凉燥犯肺：恶寒无汗、头微痛·咳嗽痰稀、鼻塞咽干
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：一条时间轴定方——邪在卫（但咳轻：桑菊／热显咽痛：银翘）→邪退阴伤（肺胃：沙参麦冬／专胃不饥：益胃／肠燥便结：增液）→虚火上灼（咽喉：养阴清肺）；贯穿两条纪律：治上焦如羽（轻剂勿久煎）、存得一分津液便有一分生机
本法系与桂林古本温病篇构成「温病双源」：古本治伏气温病（自内而发，柴胡剂改装＋凉血重剂），叶吴学派治新感温病（自外而受，卫气营血分层）——两源在“护阴”一点上完全合流。选六首勾勒新感温病的头尾：卫分两档（桑菊轻、银翘平——气分之白虎承气经方已备），善后四方（沙参麦冬／益胃／增液／养阴清肺——把“热病之后怎么收拾阴伤”做成一套）。用药零件半数是经方旧物：豆豉竹叶桔梗杏仁麦冬地黄——新学派新在分层与剂法，不在药。
法系演化树
卫分：桑叶菊花＋杏桔升降→ 桑菊饮（辛凉轻剂·但咳）──热显→ 银翘散（辛凉平剂·咽痛口渴，豆豉承栀子豉之宣）
凉燥一格：苏叶前胡轻散＋杏桔升降＋二陈→ 杏苏散（苦温甘辛法，燥忌苦两路共守）
气分（经方已备）：白虎汤 · 白虎加人参 ── 腑实：承气类 ── 温病下法原型：大黄黄芩地黄牡丹汤（古本）
邪退阴伤：麦门冬汤（经方 · 滋而兼运）──去参夏纯甘寒→ 沙参麦冬汤（肺胃）──专胃→ 益胃汤（不饥不食）
津枯肠燥：古本半攻半滋原型 ──纯滋一端→ 增液汤（增水行舟）──不下者→ 增液承气（回归半攻半滋）
虚火上灼：增液汤底盘＋贝丹薄芍→ 养阴清肺汤（虚火喉痹·白喉忌表）
※ 伏气温病参桂林古本拓展篇：三温时序 · 五脏传变 · 三焦留邪三张地图
临证决策链（抓手 → 选方 → 推理）
但咳、身热不甚、口微渴
→
桑菊饮
辛凉轻剂：邪浅热微——痰多清稀属寒者温化。
发热微恶寒、咽痛口渴、舌尖红脉浮数
→
银翘散
辛凉平剂：寒温之辨（恶寒重无汗身痛者麻桂）是外感第一辨。
燥伤肺胃：咽干口渴、干咳少痰、舌红少苔
→
沙参麦冬汤
甘寒法：与麦门冬汤以有无“火逆上气”分亚型。
热病后不饥不食、口干舌红少苔
→
益胃汤
胃阴伤之不纳：舌苔有无分食积／脾虚／阴伤三途。
津亏便秘：干结数日不行而无痞满拒按
→
增液汤
增水行舟：实积之秘当下不当滋——不下者转增液承气。
咽干喉痛缠绵、干咳、舌红少苔（虚火喉痹）
→
养阴清肺汤
白喉忌表：与银翘之风热客咽成咽痛两极——红肿急缓分途。
深秋恶寒无汗、咳嗽痰稀、咽干苔白（凉燥）
→
杏苏散
苦温甘辛法：燥分温凉是秋咳第一辨——温燥归桑杏沙麦一路。
常见误用
第一陷阱是寒温不辨：风寒误投辛凉则冰伏、风热误投麻桂则助燔——外感第一辨在恶寒轻重与咽渴有无。第二陷阱是滋腻恋邪：邪未净而早投沙麦地则闭门留寇——善后四方皆以“邪退”为前提。第三陷阱是轻剂重煎：银翘桑菊香气大出即取，久煎则轻清之性尽失——剂法即方义。第四陷阱是便秘滋攻不分：增液所主是“无水舟停”，痞满拒按之实积滋之则壅。
Formulas in This Class (7)
桑菊饮Sang Ju Yin
Sang Ju Yin
但咳、身热不甚·口微渴
银翘散Yin Qiao San
Yin Qiao San
发热无汗或有汗不畅·微恶风寒、头痛
沙参麦冬汤Sha Shen Mai Dong Tang
Sha Shen Mai Dong Tang
燥伤肺胃阴分·咽干口渴
益胃汤Yi Wei Tang
Yi Wei Tang
热病后胃阴受伤·不饥不食（饥不欲食）
增液汤Zeng Ye Tang
Zeng Ye Tang
阳明温病、津亏便秘·大便干结、数日不行而无痞满燥实之全局
养阴清肺汤Yang Yin Qing Fei Tang
Yang Yin Qing Fei Tang
阴虚燥热之咽喉肿痛（白喉及虚火喉痹）·咽干鼻燥
杏苏散Xing Su San
Xing Su San
凉燥犯肺：恶寒无汗、头微痛·咳嗽痰稀、鼻塞咽干
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: one time-axis fixes the formula — pathogen at the defensive (cough only, mild: Sang Ju / heat evident with sore throat: Yin Qiao) → pathogen retreating with yin damage (Lung-stomach: Sha Shen Mai Dong / stomach alone without hunger: Yi Wei / dry bowel bound stool: Zeng Ye) → deficiency fire scorching up (throat: Yang Yin Qing Fei); two disciplines throughout: treat the upper burner like a feather (light formulas, do not boil long), and preserving one part of fluid keeps one part of vitality
This lineage and the Guilin edition warm-disease chapters form the "twin warm-disease sources": the Guilin edition treats latent-qi warm disease (arising from within, the Chai Hu formulas remade + heavy blood-cooling doses), the Ye-Wu school treats newly-contracted warm disease (received from without, layered by defensive-qi-nutritive-blood) — the two sources converge completely on the single point of protecting yin. Six formulas are chosen to sketch the head and tail of newly-contracted warm disease: two defensive-level grades (Sang Ju light, Yin Qiao even — the qi-level Bai Hu and Cheng Qi being already provided by classical formulas), and four aftermath formulas (Sha Shen Mai Dong / Yi Wei / Zeng Ye / Yang Yin Qing Fei — making "how to clean up yin damage after a febrile disease" into one set). Half the herb-parts are classical stock: Dou Chi, Zhu Ye, Jie Geng, Xing Ren, Mai Dong, Di Huang — the new school's novelty is in layering and decoction method, not in herbs.
Lineage Evolution
Defensive level: Sang Ye-Ju Hua + Xing-Jie ascending-descending → Sang Ju Yin (acrid-cool light formula · cough only) ── heat evident → Yin Qiao San (acrid-cool even formula · sore throat and thirst, Dou Chi carrying Zhi Zi Chi's diffusion)
The cool-dryness grade: Su Ye-Qian Hu light dispersing + Xing-Jie ascending-descending + Er Chen → Xing Su San (the bitter-warm sweet-acrid method, guarding both the dryness-shuns-bitter roads)
Qi level (already provided by classical formulas): Bai Hu Tang · Bai Hu Jia Ren Shen ── bowel repletion: the Cheng Qi class ── the warm-disease purging prototype: Da Huang Huang Qin Di Huang Mu Dan Tang (Guilin edition)
Pathogen retreating with yin damage: Mai Men Dong Tang (classical · enriching with transporting) ── drop ginseng-Ban Xia to pure sweet-cold → Sha Shen Mai Dong Tang (Lung-stomach) ── stomach alone → Yi Wei Tang (no hunger, no eating)
Fluid-withered dry bowel: the Guilin edition half-attack half-enrich prototype ── to the pure-enriching end → Zeng Ye Tang (boost water to float the boat) ── if it does not move → Zeng Ye Cheng Qi (returning to half-attack half-enrich)
Deficiency fire scorching up: the Zeng Ye base + Bei Mu-Mu Dan Pi-Bo He-Shao Yao → Yang Yin Qing Fei Tang (deficiency-fire throat-obstruction · diphtheria shuns the exterior)
※ For latent-qi warm disease see the Guilin extension: three maps of three-warmth timing · five-viscera transmission · triple-burner lodged pathogen
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Cough only, body heat not severe, slight thirst
→
Sang Ju Yin
Acrid-cool light formula: shallow pathogen, mild heat — copious thin clear phlegm belonging to cold is warm-transformed.
Fever with slight aversion to cold, sore throat and thirst, red tongue-tip, floating rapid pulse
→
Yin Qiao San
Acrid-cool even formula: the cold-warm distinction (severe aversion to cold, no sweat, body pain → Ma-Gui) is the first distinction in contraction.
Dryness harming Lung-stomach: dry throat and thirst, dry cough with scant phlegm, red tongue with scant coat
→
Sha Shen Mai Dong Tang
The sweet-cold method: divided from Mai Men Dong Tang by the presence of "fire-counterflow qi rising."
After a febrile disease, no hunger and no eating, dry mouth, red tongue with scant coat
→
Yi Wei Tang
Stomach-yin damage with non-acceptance: the presence of tongue coat divides the three roads of food accumulation / Spleen deficiency / yin damage.
Fluid-depletion constipation: dry bound stool for days without focal fullness resisting pressure
→
Zeng Ye Tang
Boost water to float the boat: a replete-accumulation constipation should be purged, not enriched — if it does not move, switch to Zeng Ye Cheng Qi.
Lingering dry sore throat, dry cough, red tongue with scant coat (deficiency-fire throat obstruction)
→
Yang Yin Qing Fei Tang
Diphtheria shuns the exterior: forming with Yin Qiao's wind-heat lodged throat the two poles of sore throat — red swelling, acute or gradual, divides the roads.
Late-autumn aversion to cold without sweat, cough with thin phlegm, dry throat with white coat (cool-dryness)
→
Xing Su San
The bitter-warm sweet-acrid method: dividing dryness into warm and cool is the first distinction in autumn cough — warm-dryness goes to the Sang-Xing-Sha-Mai road.
Common Misuse
The first trap is not distinguishing cold and warm: wind-cold mistakenly given acrid-cool freezes and traps, wind-heat mistakenly given Ma-Gui abets the blaze — the first distinction in contraction is the severity of aversion to cold and the presence of thirst. The second trap is cloying and lodging the pathogen: giving Sha-Mai-Di early before the pathogen is cleared shuts the door on the bandit — the four aftermath formulas all take "pathogen retreated" as premise. The third trap is over-boiling a light formula: Yin Qiao and Sang Ju are taken the moment the aroma emerges, and long boiling loses all their light-clearing nature — the decoction method is the formula's meaning. The fourth trap is not distinguishing enriching from attacking in constipation: what Zeng Ye governs is "the boat stopped for want of water," and enriching a replete accumulation with focal fullness resisting pressure congests.
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

清利·痹证·安神Clearing / Bi / Shen Lineages

类方鉴别 · 类别枢纽Formula Class Hub
本类方剂（13）
龙胆泻肝汤Long Dan Xie Gan Tang
Long Dan Xie Gan Tang
肝胆实火上炎：头痛目赤、胁痛口苦、耳聋耳肿·或肝经湿热下注：阴肿阴痒、小便淋浊、带下黄臭
芍药汤Shao Yao Tang
Shao Yao Tang
下痢脓血、赤白相兼·腹痛、里急后重
八正散Ba Zheng San
Ba Zheng San
尿频尿急、溺时涩痛·淋沥不畅、尿色浑赤
泻白散Xie Bai San
Xie Bai San
肺热咳喘·皮肤蒸热、日晡尤甚
清胃散Qing Wei San
Qing Wei San
牙痛牵引头脑、面颊发热·齿龈红肿溃烂或出血
玉女煎Yu Nv Jian
Yu Nv Jian
牙痛齿松、烦热干渴·头痛、齿龈出血（衄）
萆薢分清饮Bi Xie Fen Qing Yin
Bi Xie Fen Qing Yin
小便频数浑浊、白如米泔·凝如膏糊（膏淋、白浊）
蠲痹汤Juan Bi Tang
Juan Bi Tang
肩项臂痛、举动艰难·风寒湿痹之上半身证
宣痹汤Xuan Bi Tang
Xuan Bi Tang
湿热痹：关节红肿灼热疼痛·痛处焮热、得凉稍舒
独活寄生汤Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang
Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang
痹证日久、腰膝冷痛·肢节屈伸不利、麻木不仁
天王补心丹Tian Wang Bu Xin Dan
Tian Wang Bu Xin Dan
虚烦失眠、心悸神疲·健忘、梦遗
安神定志丸An Shen Ding Zhi Wan
An Shen Ding Zhi Wan
心胆气虚之惊悸·夜寐易惊、梦中惊跳
交泰丸Jiao Tai Wan
Jiao Tai Wan
心肾不交之失眠·心悸不宁、怔忡
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：三个小法系合卡——清利按“火湿之地”选方（肝胆经：龙胆泻肝／肠：芍药汤／膀胱：八正散／肺：泻白散／胃：清胃散·玉女煎／虚寒白浊反例：萆薢分清）；痹证按“病程×寒热”定位（邪盛：蠲痹／瘀阻：身痛逐瘀／正虚：独活寄生／湿热：宣痹）；安神按“虚之何在”分格（阴虚火扰：补心丹／气虚胆怯：定志丸／水火不交：交泰丸）
本卡收三个中等规模法系。清利七方是“脏腑定位学”的操练场：同一个火与湿热，落在肝胆、肠、膀胱、肺、胃各有专方——症状沿哪条经络脏腑分布，方就在哪里；萆薢分清饮是刻意安排的反例（同在水道而寒热相反），提醒清利非万能。痹证四方与逐瘀法系之身痛逐瘀共同拼出痹证完整地图：横轴寒热（温散诸方 vs 宣痹）、纵轴病程（蠲痹→身痛逐瘀→独活寄生），羌独上下分工、桂枝芍药知母汤为其经方之祖。安神三方补齐不寐惊悸八格地图：经方五格（郁热栀子豉／火亢黄连阿胶／血虚酸枣仁／阳浮龙牡／脏躁甘麦）＋时方三格（痰扰温胆·心脾归脾已在他卡；此卡收阴虚补心丹／气怯定志丸／不交交泰丸）。
法系演化树
清利支：黄芩汤（经方热利之祖）→ 芍药汤（调气行血十四字纲领）；猪苓汤 ──撤护阴重装→ 八正散；泻心苦折＋火郁发之→ 清胃散；白虎＋地黄法系→ 玉女煎（古本白虎加地黄之后身）；肺之柔清→ 泻白散；反例：肾气丸温摄一路→ 萆薢分清饮（寒浊）
痹证支：桂枝芍药知母汤（经方历节 · 攻养同炉之祖）──邪盛期→ 蠲痹汤（芪桂五物之祛邪扩编 · 羌活主上）──瘀阻期→ 身痛逐瘀汤（见逐瘀法系）──正虚期→ 独活寄生汤（独活主下 · 四祛三补八养）；湿热一格→ 宣痹汤（承麻杏薏甘 · 三焦分消）
安神支：黄连阿胶汤（泻南补北之祖）──滋养化→ 天王补心丹；──枢机化→ 交泰丸（连十桂一）；龙牡镇潜──虚怯再分档→ 安神定志丸（补为主镇为佐）
临证决策链（抓手 → 选方 → 推理）
目赤耳肿胁痛口苦，或阴痒淋浊带下（沿肝经分布＋脉弦数有力）
→
龙胆泻肝汤
肝胆实火湿热上下两窜：折利养达四手——脉力与丹栀逍遥分档。
下痢赤白、腹痛里急后重、肛门灼热
→
芍药汤
调气行血十四字：与黄芩汤（轻）白头翁（毒）三分热利——痢禁早涩。
尿频急涩痛、色浑赤（热淋盛实）
→
八正散
纯攻之利：与猪苓汤（兼阴伤）分虚实——中病即止。
肺热咳喘、皮肤蒸热日晡甚（小儿尤宜）
→
泻白散
清肺柔剂：伏火郁热——邪盛之喘归麻杏石甘。
牙痛齿龈红肿溃烂、口臭、脉滑数（实火）
→
清胃散
连折＋升麻火郁发之——与玉女煎以肿烂/松动分虚实。
牙痛齿松、烦热干渴、脉洪大重按无力（水亏火盛）
→
玉女煎
清滋并进：膏地同锅——古本白虎加地黄之后身。
小便浑浊白如米泔、不痛、舌淡（虚寒白浊）
→
萆薢分清饮
清利之反例：温化分清——尿色痛感舌象三对指标定寒热。
肩项臂痛举动艰难（上半身痹、邪盛正微虚）
→
蠲痹汤
羌活主上＋姜黄横行肩臂——治风先治血、芪防固卫。
关节红肿灼热痛、得凉稍舒（湿热痹）
→
宣痹汤
痹之热型：三焦分消不用风药——红肿与否是痹证第一眼。
痹久腰膝冷痛、麻木、心悸气短脉细弱（正虚邪恋）
→
独活寄生汤
病程末端：四祛三补八养——初起邪盛勿早补。
虚烦失眠心悸、手足心热、口舌生疮、舌红少苔
→
天王补心丹
心肾阴虚：泻南补北之滋养版——痰火苔腻者归温胆。
夜寐易惊、胆怯善恐、舌淡脉弱
→
安神定志丸
气虚胆怯之惊：补为主镇为佐——与温胆之痰惊虚实分途。
失眠心悸、上热口疮而下寒足冷（心肾不交）
→
交泰丸
连十桂一水火交泰：枢机之不交——阴亏舌绛者归黄连阿胶。
常见误用
清利支的总陷阱是“苦寒无度”：全支皆攻伐之品，中病即止、护阴护胃两条纪律贯穿（龙胆泻肝自带归地、八正散明令勿久服）。痹证支的陷阱是坐标错位：寒热错（湿热痹投温散如火上浇油）与病程错（邪盛早补则闭门留寇、正虚纯攻则愈攻愈痛）。安神支的陷阱是虚实痰火不分：惊悸失眠四问——苔腻否（痰）、舌红少苔否（阴虚）、舌淡脉弱否（气怯）、足冷口疮并见否（不交），一问一格勿混。
Formulas in This Class (13)
龙胆泻肝汤Long Dan Xie Gan Tang
Long Dan Xie Gan Tang
肝胆实火上炎：头痛目赤、胁痛口苦、耳聋耳肿·或肝经湿热下注：阴肿阴痒、小便淋浊、带下黄臭
芍药汤Shao Yao Tang
Shao Yao Tang
下痢脓血、赤白相兼·腹痛、里急后重
八正散Ba Zheng San
Ba Zheng San
尿频尿急、溺时涩痛·淋沥不畅、尿色浑赤
泻白散Xie Bai San
Xie Bai San
肺热咳喘·皮肤蒸热、日晡尤甚
清胃散Qing Wei San
Qing Wei San
牙痛牵引头脑、面颊发热·齿龈红肿溃烂或出血
玉女煎Yu Nv Jian
Yu Nv Jian
牙痛齿松、烦热干渴·头痛、齿龈出血（衄）
萆薢分清饮Bi Xie Fen Qing Yin
Bi Xie Fen Qing Yin
小便频数浑浊、白如米泔·凝如膏糊（膏淋、白浊）
蠲痹汤Juan Bi Tang
Juan Bi Tang
肩项臂痛、举动艰难·风寒湿痹之上半身证
宣痹汤Xuan Bi Tang
Xuan Bi Tang
湿热痹：关节红肿灼热疼痛·痛处焮热、得凉稍舒
独活寄生汤Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang
Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang
痹证日久、腰膝冷痛·肢节屈伸不利、麻木不仁
天王补心丹Tian Wang Bu Xin Dan
Tian Wang Bu Xin Dan
虚烦失眠、心悸神疲·健忘、梦遗
安神定志丸An Shen Ding Zhi Wan
An Shen Ding Zhi Wan
心胆气虚之惊悸·夜寐易惊、梦中惊跳
交泰丸Jiao Tai Wan
Jiao Tai Wan
心肾不交之失眠·心悸不宁、怔忡
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: three small lineages combined on one card — clear-benefit chooses by "the ground of fire and damp" (Liver-Gallbladder channel: Long Dan Xie Gan / intestine: Shao Yao Tang / bladder: Ba Zheng San / Lung: Xie Bai San / stomach: Qing Wei San · Yu Nu Jian / deficiency-cold white-turbidity counter-example: Bi Xie Fen Qing); bi patterns are located by "course × cold-heat" (exuberant pathogen: Juan Bi / stasis obstruction: Shen Tong Zhu Yu / upright deficiency: Du Huo Ji Sheng / damp-heat: Xuan Bi); spirit-calming is gridded by "where the deficiency lies" (yin-deficiency fire-harassing: Bu Xin Dan / qi-deficiency timidity: Ding Zhi Wan / water-fire non-interaction: Jiao Tai Wan)
This card gathers three medium-scale lineages. The seven clear-benefit formulas are a drill-ground of "visceral localization": the same fire and damp-heat, falling on the Liver-Gallbladder, intestine, bladder, Lung or stomach, each has its specialist formula — wherever the symptoms distribute along the channel and viscus, there the formula lies; Bi Xie Fen Qing Yin is a deliberately placed counter-example (same water passage but opposite in cold-heat), reminding that clear-benefit is not universal. The four bi formulas with the stasis-lineage's Shen Tong Zhu Yu together assemble a complete bi map: the horizontal axis cold-heat (the warm-dispersing formulas vs Xuan Bi), the vertical axis disease-course (Juan Bi → Shen Tong Zhu Yu → Du Huo Ji Sheng), Qiang Huo and Du Huo dividing upper and lower, Gui Zhi Shao Yao Zhi Mu Tang their classical ancestor. The three spirit-calming formulas complete the eight-grid map of sleeplessness and fright-palpitation: the classical five grids (constrained-heat Zhi Zi Chi / fire-hyperactive Huang Lian E Jiao / blood-deficiency Suan Zao Ren / yang-floating Long-Mu / visceral-agitation Gan Mai Da Zao) + three later grids (phlegm-harassing Wen Dan and Heart-Spleen Gui Pi are on other cards; this card gathers yin-deficiency Bu Xin Dan / qi-timidity Ding Zhi Wan / non-interaction Jiao Tai Wan).
Lineage Evolution
Clear-benefit branch: Huang Qin Tang (classical ancestor of heat-diarrhea) → Shao Yao Tang (the fourteen-character dictum of regulating qi and moving blood); Zhu Ling Tang ── strip yin-protection, up-armor → Ba Zheng San; the Xie Xin bitter-cutting + venting fire-constraint → Qing Wei San; Bai Hu + the Dihuang lineage → Yu Nu Jian (the successor of the Guilin edition Bai Hu Jia Di Huang); the Lung's soft-clearing → Xie Bai San; the counter-example: the Shen Qi Wan warm-restraining road → Bi Xie Fen Qing Yin (cold-turbidity)
Bi branch: Gui Zhi Shao Yao Zhi Mu Tang (classical joint-running · the ancestor of attack-and-nourish in one) ── exuberant-pathogen phase → Juan Bi Tang (an expansion of Huang Qi Gui Zhi Wu Wu Tang's pathogen-expelling · Qiang Huo governing the upper) ── stasis-obstruction phase → Shen Tong Zhu Yu Tang (see the stasis-expelling lineage) ── upright-deficiency phase → Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang (Du Huo governing the lower · four-expel three-supplement eight-nourish); the damp-heat grid → Xuan Bi Tang (carrying Ma Xing Yi Gan · triple-burner separation)
Spirit-calming branch: Huang Lian E Jiao Tang (the ancestor of draining the south, supplementing the north) ── to enrich-nourish → Tian Wang Bu Xin Dan; ── to pivot → Jiao Tai Wan (Huang Lian ten, Rou Gui one); Long-Mu subduing ── deficiency-timidity further graded → An Shen Ding Zhi Wan (supplement predominant, subdue as assistant)
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Red eyes, ear swelling, hypochondriac pain, bitter taste, or genital itch, turbid strangury and discharge (distributed along the Liver channel + wiry rapid forceful pulse)
→
Long Dan Xie Gan Tang
Liver-Gallbladder repletion-fire and damp-heat darting up and down: cutting, benefiting, nourishing and reaching, four hands — divided from Dan Zhi Xiao Yao by pulse strength.
Red-white dysentery, abdominal pain with heaviness below, scorching anus
→
Shao Yao Tang
The fourteen-character dictum of regulating qi and moving blood: a three-way division of heat-diarrhea with Huang Qin Tang (light) and Bai Tou Weng (toxin) — dysentery forbids early astringing.
Frequent urgent painful urination, turbid red color (heat-strangury exuberant repletion)
→
Ba Zheng San
Pure-attack benefiting: dividing deficiency and repletion with Zhu Ling Tang (with yin damage) — stop once it hits the mark.
Lung-heat cough-wheeze, steaming skin heat worse in the late afternoon (especially apt for children)
→
Xie Bai San
The Lung-clearing soft formula: latent-fire constrained heat — exuberant-pathogen wheeze goes to Ma Xing Shi Gan.
Toothache with red swollen ulcerated gums, foul breath, slippery rapid pulse (repletion fire)
→
Qing Wei San
Huang Lian cutting + Sheng Ma venting fire-constraint — divided from Yu Nu Jian by swelling-ulceration versus looseness (deficiency).
Toothache with loose teeth, vexing heat and dry thirst, surging pulse forceless on heavy pressure (water depleted, fire exuberant)
→
Yu Nv Jian
Clearing and enriching together: Shi Gao and Di Huang in one pot — the successor of the Guilin edition Bai Hu Jia Di Huang.
Turbid urine white as rice-water, painless, pale tongue (deficiency-cold white-turbidity)
→
Bi Xie Fen Qing Yin
The counter-example of clear-benefit: warm-transforming to separate the clear — urine color, pain sensation and tongue, three paired indicators fixing cold-heat.
Shoulder-nape-arm pain with difficult movement (upper-body bi, exuberant pathogen with slight upright deficiency)
→
Juan Bi Tang
Qiang Huo governing the upper + Jiang Huang traveling across the shoulder and arm — treat wind first treat blood, Huang Qi-Fang Feng consolidating the defensive.
Red swollen scorching painful joints, slightly eased by cold (damp-heat bi)
→
Xuan Bi Tang
The heat type of bi: triple-burner separation without wind herbs — red swelling or not is the first glance in bi patterns.
Chronic bi with cold aching lower back and knees, numbness, palpitation, shortness of breath, thin weak pulse (upright deficiency with lingering pathogen)
→
Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang
The end of the disease course: four-expel three-supplement eight-nourish — do not supplement early in the exuberant-pathogen onset.
Deficiency-vexation insomnia, palpitation, hot palms and soles, mouth and tongue sores, red tongue with scant coat
→
Tian Wang Bu Xin Dan
Heart-Kidney yin deficiency: the enrich-nourish version of draining the south, supplementing the north — phlegm-fire with greasy coat goes to Wen Dan.
Easy fright in sleep, timid and fearful, pale tongue, weak pulse
→
An Shen Ding Zhi Wan
The fright of qi-deficiency timidity: supplement predominant, subdue as assistant — divided from Wen Dan's phlegm-fright by deficiency and repletion.
Insomnia, palpitation, heat above with mouth sores and cold below with cold feet (Heart-Kidney non-interaction)
→
Jiao Tai Wan
Huang Lian ten, Rou Gui one, water and fire interacting in peace: the non-interaction of the pivot — yin-depletion with crimson tongue goes to Huang Lian E Jiao.
Common Misuse
The general trap of the clear-benefit branch is "boundless bitter-cold": the whole branch is attacking herbs, and the two disciplines of stopping once it hits the mark and protecting yin and stomach run throughout (Long Dan Xie Gan carries its own Dang Gui-Di Huang, Ba Zheng San explicitly forbids long use). The bi branch's trap is coordinate mismatch: cold-heat error (giving warm-dispersing to damp-heat bi is pouring oil on fire) and course error (early supplementation in the exuberant-pathogen phase shuts the door on the bandit, pure attack in the upright-deficiency phase makes attack ever more painful). The spirit-calming branch's trap is not distinguishing deficiency, repletion and phlegm-fire: four questions for fright-palpitation sleeplessness — greasy coat (phlegm), red tongue with scant coat (yin deficiency), pale tongue with weak pulse (qi timidity), cold feet with mouth sores (non-interaction), one question one grid, do not confuse.
