固摄·消导·调冲Consolidating / Digestive Lineages

类方鉴别 · 类别枢纽Formula Class Hub
本类方剂（8）
二仙汤Er Xian Tang
Er Xian Tang
更年期综合征：烘热汗出与畏寒并见·头晕耳鸣、腰酸乏力
四神丸Si Shen Wan
Si Shen Wan
五更泄泻（黎明前脐腹作痛、肠鸣即泻、泻后则安）·久泻不愈
真人养脏汤Zhen Ren Yang Zang Tang
Zhen Ren Yang Zang Tang
久泻久痢、滑脱不禁·甚则脱肛坠下
缩泉丸Suo Quan Wan
Suo Quan Wan
小便频数·小儿遗尿
生化汤Sheng Hua Tang
Sheng Hua Tang
产后恶露不行或行而不畅·小腹冷痛
保和丸Bao He Wan
Bao He Wan
脘腹痞满胀痛·嗳腐吞酸、恶食呕逆
藿香正气散Huo Xiang Zheng Qi San
Huo Xiang Zheng Qi San
外感风寒、内伤湿滞·恶寒发热、头痛
平胃散Ping Wei San
Ping Wei San
脘腹胀满·不思饮食、口淡无味
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：固摄支按“脱之何物何度”选方（定时之泻温固：四神／滑脱之泻涩固：真人养脏／尿频遗溺温摄：缩泉／阴阳两虚之冲任：二仙／产后瘀阻：生化）；消导支按“滞之轻重与兼夹”（食积平剂：保和／湿滞：平胃／寒湿表里同病：藿香正气）；总纪律一条——涩法消法皆治标，邪未净禁涩、积既去勿消
本卡收时方层最后两个法系。固摄支的宪法是“涩法是最后一道闸”：桃花汤（经方涩肠之祖）开此门，四神丸（温固）与真人养脏（涩固）按“脱之程度”分档，缩泉（尿）生化（产后血）二仙（冲任）各守一路——早涩闭邪之戒（芍药汤页立）贯穿全支。消导支的三方是中焦三张底盘的最后一块：四君（虚）、二陈（痰）、平胃（湿）——保和承二陈、藿香正气合平胃二陈双底盘再加芳香一翼。全站十二法系至此收官：时方63首各归其宗，每方之上皆可指认经方之根。
法系演化树
固摄支：桃花汤（经方 · 涩肠固脱之祖）──温固档→ 四神丸（五更泻 · 补火暖土）──涩固档→ 真人养脏汤（滑脱之闸 · 木香防壅）
肾气丸温摄一路 ──轻装→ 缩泉丸（一摄一行）；温经汤 ──产后特化→ 生化汤（化瘀生新 · 炮姜守血）
乌梅丸/附子泻心寒热同炉家法 ──现代应用→ 二仙汤（更年期 · 三温二清）
消导支：二陈底盘＋三消（楂曲莱菔）→ 保和丸（食积平剂）
古本白术茯苓厚朴汤 ──定型放大→ 平胃散（湿滞祖方 · 苍朴陈草）
平胃＋二陈双底盘＋藿苏芷芳香翼→ 藿香正气散（寒湿表里双解）
临证决策链（抓手 → 选方 → 推理）
烘热汗出与畏寒并见、腰酸烦躁（围绝经期）
→
二仙汤
阴阳两虚虚火浮：三温二清同锅——单纯阴虚或阳虚各归其方。
黎明定时脐痛肠鸣即泻、泻后安、畏寒
→
四神丸
命门火衰之五更泻：补火暖土，临睡服药赶在五更前布防。
久泻久痢滑脱不禁、甚则脱肛、无里急后重实象
→
真人养脏汤
滑脱之闸：涩固重剂——邪未净禁涩是铁律。
小便频数、小儿遗尿、下元虚冷
→
缩泉丸
温摄轻剂：一摄一行——湿热尿频（灼痛）归八正。
产后恶露不行、小腹冷痛、块紫暗
→
生化汤
化瘀生新：归重姜黑——血热恶露臭秽者禁温。
脘痞嗳腐吞酸、泻下臭如败卵、苔厚腻
→
保和丸
食积平剂：三消分工（肉/酒/谷）——脾虚无积者当补。
恶寒发热＋呕吐泄泻（胃肠型感冒、水土不服）
→
藿香正气散
寒湿表里双解：芳香化浊——暑热无湿者禁香燥。
脘腹胀满、口淡纳呆、肢重、苔白厚腻
→
平胃散
湿滞祖方：燥湿行气两轮——苔腻厚度即刻度尺。
常见误用
固摄支的天条是“早涩闭邪”：泻痢初起有灼热臭秽里急后重者一律禁涩禁温固——湿热未净而落闸等于把贼关在屋里。消导支的对偶陷阱是“消伐无度”：消法伤正，积去即止；脾虚之纳呆（无积滞实象）误消则愈虚——虚痞与实滞以嗳腐苔厚有无分界。二仙汤专属提醒：烘热与畏寒必须并见方用，见热投凉见寒投温皆落单边。
Formulas in This Class (8)
二仙汤Er Xian Tang
Er Xian Tang
更年期综合征：烘热汗出与畏寒并见·头晕耳鸣、腰酸乏力
四神丸Si Shen Wan
Si Shen Wan
五更泄泻（黎明前脐腹作痛、肠鸣即泻、泻后则安）·久泻不愈
真人养脏汤Zhen Ren Yang Zang Tang
Zhen Ren Yang Zang Tang
久泻久痢、滑脱不禁·甚则脱肛坠下
缩泉丸Suo Quan Wan
Suo Quan Wan
小便频数·小儿遗尿
生化汤Sheng Hua Tang
Sheng Hua Tang
产后恶露不行或行而不畅·小腹冷痛
保和丸Bao He Wan
Bao He Wan
脘腹痞满胀痛·嗳腐吞酸、恶食呕逆
藿香正气散Huo Xiang Zheng Qi San
Huo Xiang Zheng Qi San
外感风寒、内伤湿滞·恶寒发热、头痛
平胃散Ping Wei San
Ping Wei San
脘腹胀满·不思饮食、口淡无味
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: the consolidating branch chooses by "what is deserting and to what degree" (timed diarrhea warm-consolidated: Si Shen / slippage-diarrhea astringe-consolidated: Zhen Ren Yang Zang / urinary frequency and enuresis warm-restrained: Suo Quan / yin-yang dual-deficiency Chong-Ren: Er Xian / postpartum stasis-obstruction: Sheng Hua); the digestive branch by "the severity and accompaniment of stagnation" (food accumulation even formula: Bao He / damp-stagnation: Ping Wei / cold-damp exterior-interior disease: Huo Xiang Zheng Qi); one general discipline — both astringing and digestive methods treat the branch, forbid astringing before the pathogen is cleared, do not digest once the accumulation is gone
This card gathers the last two lineages of the later-formula layer. The consolidating branch's constitution is "astringing is the last floodgate": Tao Hua Tang (the classical ancestor of astringing the intestine) opens this gate, Si Shen Wan (warm-consolidating) and Zhen Ren Yang Zang (astringe-consolidating) grade by "degree of desertion," while Suo Quan (urine), Sheng Hua (postpartum blood) and Er Xian (Chong-Ren) each guard a road — the caution against early astringing shutting in the pathogen (set on the Shao Yao Tang page) runs through the whole branch. The digestive branch's three formulas are the last piece of the three middle-burner bases: Si Jun (deficiency), Er Chen (phlegm), Ping Wei (damp) — Bao He carries Er Chen, Huo Xiang Zheng Qi combines the Ping Wei and Er Chen double base plus an aromatic wing. The whole twelve-lineage station closes here: the 63 later formulas each return to their source, and above every formula a classical root can be identified.
Lineage Evolution
Consolidating branch: Tao Hua Tang (classical · ancestor of astringing the intestine and consolidating desertion) ── warm-consolidate grade → Si Shen Wan (fifth-watch diarrhea · supplement fire to warm earth) ── astringe-consolidate grade → Zhen Ren Yang Zang Tang (the floodgate of slippage · Mu Xiang preventing congestion)
The Shen Qi Wan warm-restraining road ── lightly armed → Suo Quan Wan (one restraining, one moving); Wen Jing Tang ── postpartum specialization → Sheng Hua Tang (transform stasis, engender new · blast-fried ginger guarding blood)
The Wu Mei Wan / Fu Zi Xie Xin cold-heat-in-one-pot house method ── modern application → Er Xian Tang (menopause · three warm, two clear)
Digestive branch: the Er Chen base + three digestants (Shan Zha-Shen Qu-Lai Fu Zi) → Bao He Wan (food-accumulation even formula)
The Guilin edition Bai Zhu Fu Ling Hou Po Tang ── settled and amplified → Ping Wei San (the ancestral damp-stagnation formula · Cang Zhu-Hou Po-Chen Pi-Gan Cao)
Ping Wei + Er Chen double base + Huo Xiang-Su Ye-Bai Zhi aromatic wing → Huo Xiang Zheng Qi San (cold-damp exterior-interior resolution)
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Flushing heat with sweating and aversion to cold together, aching lower back, vexation (perimenopause)
→
Er Xian Tang
Yin-yang dual deficiency with deficiency-fire floating: three warm, two clear in one pot — pure yin deficiency or yang deficiency each return to their formula.
Timed pre-dawn periumbilical pain with borborygmus then diarrhea, calm after, aversion to cold
→
Si Shen Wan
Fifth-watch diarrhea of Ming Men fire debilitation: supplement fire to warm earth, taken at bedtime to defend before the fifth watch.
Chronic diarrhea-dysentery with unrestrained slippage, even rectal prolapse, no sign of heaviness or replete accumulation
→
Zhen Ren Yang Zang Tang
The floodgate of slippage: an astringe-consolidate heavy formula — astringing before the pathogen is cleared is the iron law forbidden.
Frequent urination, childhood enuresis, lower-origin deficiency-cold
→
Suo Quan Wan
A warm-restraining light formula: one restraining, one moving — damp-heat urinary frequency (scorching pain) goes to Ba Zheng.
Retained postpartum lochia, cold lower-abdomen pain, dark purple clots
→
Sheng Hua Tang
Transform stasis, engender new: Dang Gui heavy, ginger charred — forbidden to warm blood-heat lochia with foul smell.
Epigastric focal fullness with rotten belching and acid swallowing, foul rotten-egg diarrhea, thick greasy coat
→
Bao He Wan
The food-accumulation even formula: three digestants divided (meat / wine / grain) — Spleen deficiency without accumulation should be supplemented.
Aversion to cold with fever + vomiting and diarrhea (gastrointestinal-type cold, unaccustomed conditions)
→
Huo Xiang Zheng Qi San
Cold-damp exterior-interior resolution: aromatic transforming turbidity — summer-heat without damp forbids aromatic-drying.
Epigastric-abdominal distension, bland mouth, poor appetite, heavy limbs, white thick greasy coat
→
Ping Wei San
The ancestral damp-stagnation formula: drying damp and moving qi, two wheels — the thickness of the greasy coat is itself the measuring stick.
Common Misuse
The consolidating branch's cardinal rule is "early astringing shuts in the pathogen": for early diarrhea-dysentery with scorching, foul smell and heaviness below, astringing and warm-consolidating are categorically forbidden — dropping the floodgate before the damp-heat is cleared is locking the thief in the house. The digestive branch's dual trap is "boundless digesting-attacking": the digestive method harms the upright, stop once the accumulation is gone; the poor appetite of Spleen deficiency (no sign of replete accumulation) mistakenly digested grows more deficient — deficiency focal-fullness and replete stagnation divide by the presence of belching of rotten food and a thick coat. Er Xian Tang's specific reminder: flushing heat and aversion to cold must appear together to use it, giving cooling for heat or warming for cold alone falls to one side.
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

麻黄 · 桂枝 · 细辛Ma Huang / Gui Zhi / Xi Xin

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：同属辛温开“出”，开的层次不同——皮毛 / 肌腠血脉 / 深层伏寒
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
无汗表实、毛窍闭塞
→
麻黄
开最外层的门（毛窍）。力峻而专，有汗者用之则漏。
有汗表虚、营卫不和；或阳气不振于心脉
→
桂枝
不破门而温通：走肌腠血脉，助卫阳布达、心阳升动。麻黄治“不通”，桂枝治“不和”。
寒伏少阴、水饮沉痼、深部冷痛
→
细辛
从最深层向外透：起少阴之伏寒、化肺中之冷饮——从阴出阳之药。
临证要点
三药常同方（如小青龙、麻辛附），并非重复，是三个深度上同时开路。临证配伍时按病位深浅取舍：表闭无里者不需细辛，里寒无表者不需麻黄。
本组成员
麻黄桂枝细辛
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: all acrid-warm openers of "exiting," but opening at different layers — skin-and-hair / flesh-interstices and blood vessels / deep lurking cold
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
No sweat, exterior repletion, pores closed
→
Ma Huang
Opens the outermost door (the pores). Fierce and focused; used with sweat, it leaks.
Sweating, exterior deficiency, nutritive-defensive disharmony; or yang qi not roused in the Heart vessels
→
Gui Zhi
Warms and frees without breaking the door: travels the flesh-interstices and blood vessels, aiding defensive-yang spread and Heart-yang rousing. Ma Huang treats "blockage," Gui Zhi treats "disharmony."
Cold lurking in Shao Yin, deep-lodged water-rheum, deep cold pain
→
Xi Xin
Vents from the deepest layer outward: rousing Shao Yin's lurking cold and transforming cold rheum in the Lung — an herb of exiting yang from within yin.
Clinical Key Points
The three herbs often share a formula (as in Xiao Qing Long, Ma-Xin-Fu); this is not redundancy but opening the road at three depths at once. In clinical combination, choose by disease depth: exterior closure without an interior needs no Xi Xin; interior cold without an exterior needs no Ma Huang.
Members of This Group
Ma HuangGui ZhiXi Xin
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

生姜 · 干姜Sheng Jiang / Gan Jiang

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：走与守——同源之姜，鲜者走散、干者守温
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
表寒需散、胃中停饮上逆作呕
→
生姜
走而不守：向上向外，散表寒、开胃滞、化呕逆——动态之姜。
中焦虚寒之吐利冷痛、肺寒冷饮
→
干姜
守而不走：蹲守中焦专温脾阳，兼温肺化饮——静态之姜。中阳是升降之轴，故理中、四逆皆倚之。
临证要点
呕而误用干姜（应生姜）则壅，中寒误用生姜（应干姜）则散而无功。炮姜则更进一层：守中之外兼能止血，温经汤法中见之（后批详展）。一物三姜，炮制即是药性再设计。
本组成员
生姜干姜
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: traveling versus guarding — same-source ginger, fresh travels and disperses, dried guards and warms
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Exterior cold needing dispersal, lodged stomach rheum counterflowing into retching
→
Sheng Jiang
Travels and does not guard: upward and outward, dispersing exterior cold, opening stomach stagnation, transforming retching-counterflow — the dynamic ginger.
Middle-burner deficiency-cold with vomiting-diarrhea and cold pain, Lung-cold rheum
→
Gan Jiang
Guards and does not travel: crouching in the middle burner to warm the Spleen yang specifically, also warming the Lung and transforming rheum — the static ginger. The middle yang being the axle of ascending-descending, Li Zhong and Si Ni both rely on it.
Clinical Key Points
Mistakenly using Gan Jiang for retching (which needs Sheng Jiang) congests; mistakenly using Sheng Jiang for middle-cold (which needs Gan Jiang) disperses without effect. Blast-fried ginger goes a step further: beyond guarding the middle it also stops bleeding, seen in the Wen Jing Tang method. One plant, three gingers — processing is a redesign of the herb's nature.
Members of This Group
Sheng JiangGan Jiang
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

芍药之酸收 · 桂枝之辛散Shao Yao / Gui Zhi (Yin-Yang Pair)

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：一对阴阳砝码——出与入、散与收、卫与营
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
营阴外泄（汗出）、筋脉拘急、腹中挛痛
→
芍药
酸收之力向内向下：敛营入脉、养血柔筋。病在“收不住”“濡不到”时加码。
卫阳不振、寒凝血脉、阳虚冲逆
→
桂枝
辛温之力向外向上：助卫达表、温通心脉。病在“布不出”“温不够”时加码。
临证要点
胸满去芍（阴柔碍阳）、腹痛倍芍（酸收缓急）、奔豚加桂（温阳镇冲）——桂芍的加减是整个桂枝汤类方的操作系统。记不清某类方组成时，先想病机偏向哪端，砝码方向自明。
本组成员
芍药桂枝
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: a yin-yang pair of weights — exiting and entering, dispersing and gathering, defensive and nutritive
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Nutritive yin leaking out (sweating), cramping sinews, contracting abdominal pain
→
Shao Yao
Its sour-gathering force is inward and downward: gathering the nutritive into the vessels, nourishing blood and softening sinews. Add weight when the disease is "cannot hold in" or "cannot moisten to."
Defensive yang not roused, cold-congealed blood vessels, yang-deficiency surging counterflow
→
Gui Zhi
Its acrid-warm force is outward and upward: aiding the defensive to reach the exterior, warming and freeing the Heart vessels. Add weight when the disease is "cannot spread out" or "not warm enough."
Clinical Key Points
For chest fullness drop Shao Yao (its yin-softness hinders yang), for abdominal pain double Shao Yao (sour-gathering relieves urgency), for running piglet add Gui Zhi (warm yang, subdue the surge) — the Gui-Shao modification is the operating system of the whole Gui Zhi Tang class. When unsure of a class member's composition, first think which way the pathomechanism tips, and the weight-direction is clear.
Members of This Group
Shao YaoGui Zhi
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

附子 · 干姜（附：肉桂）Fu Zi / Gan Jiang

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：温阳之部位与动静——下焦之根 / 中焦之轴
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
四肢厥逆、脉微欲绝、但欲寐——阳气之根将熄
→
附子
走而不守，直入少阴起垂绝之阳，通行十二经。回阳救逆非彼莫属。
吐利冷痛、食不化——中轴之火不温
→
干姜
守而不走，专温脾胃。中阳复则清升浊降自还。
临证要点
“附子无干姜不热”：四逆汤中二药必偕行——附子起阳于下，干姜续火于中，走者得守者接应，其力乃彰。后世肉桂（官桂）则另司引火归元、温营血分之职，与桂枝一树两药、走守各异，用时勿混。
本组成员
附子干姜
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: the location and dynamism of warming yang — the root in the lower burner / the axle in the middle burner
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Reversal cold of the four limbs, faint pulse verging on expiry, desire only to sleep — the root of yang qi about to extinguish
→
Fu Zi
Travels and does not guard, entering Shao Yin directly to raise the about-to-expire yang, passing through all twelve channels. For restoring yang and stemming reversal, nothing else will do.
Vomiting-diarrhea with cold pain, undigested food — the fire of the central axle unwarmed
→
Gan Jiang
Guards and does not travel, warming the Spleen-Stomach specifically. Middle yang restored, the clear rises and the turbid descends of themselves.
Clinical Key Points
"Fu Zi without Gan Jiang is not hot": in Si Ni Tang the two must go together — Fu Zi raises yang below, Gan Jiang keeps the fire in the middle, and the traveler gains a guardian to answer it, its force then manifest. Later, Rou Gui takes the separate offices of guiding fire back to the origin and warming the nutritive blood level; of one tree with Gui Zhi but each traveling or guarding differently — do not confuse them in use.
Members of This Group
Fu ZiGan Jiang
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

柴胡 · 黄芩Chai Hu / Huang Qin

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：枢机之治的两翼——外透其郁 vs 内清其热
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
郁在枢机、气机不得转（往来寒热、胸胁苦满、默默不欲食）
→
柴胡
升散透达：把卡在半表半里的邪向外向上转出去。治“郁”之药。
郁已化热、热踞胆腑（口苦、心烦、热象显）
→
黄芩
苦寒沉降：从内清泄胆腑分野之热。治“热”之药。
临证要点
二药几乎不单飞——郁久必化热，清热不解郁则热复生，透郁不清热则热无制。故柴胡剂中柴芩总是并肩，问题只在比例：郁重热轻则柴重（小柴胡柴胡半斤），热重于郁则芩之份量与配伍升级（黄芩汤则径以芩为君，后批详展）。
本组成员
柴胡黄芩
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: the two wings of pivot treatment — venting its constraint outward vs clearing its heat within
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Constraint at the pivot, the qi dynamic unable to turn (alternating chills and fever, chest-hypochondrium fullness, silent unwillingness to eat)
→
Chai Hu
Ascending-dispersing and venting: turning the pathogen stuck at the half-exterior half-interior outward and upward. The herb that treats "constraint."
Constraint already transformed into heat, heat occupying the Gallbladder-bowel (bitter taste, vexation, evident heat signs)
→
Huang Qin
Bitter-cold and sinking-descending: clearing and draining the Gallbladder-bowel domain's heat from within. The herb that treats "heat."
Clinical Key Points
The two herbs almost never fly solo — long constraint must transform into heat, clearing heat without resolving constraint lets the heat regenerate, venting constraint without clearing heat leaves the heat unchecked. So in Chai Hu formulas Chai Hu and Huang Qin are always shoulder to shoulder, the question only of ratio: heavy constraint light heat, Chai Hu heavy (Xiao Chai Hu's half a jin of Chai Hu); heat heavier than constraint, Huang Qin's proportion and combination escalate (Huang Qin Tang takes Huang Qin as sovereign outright).
Members of This Group
Chai HuHuang Qin
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

枳实 · 厚朴Zhi Shi / Hou Po

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：降气药之点与面——破结之凿 vs 除满之刨
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
气滞已凝成“结”：心下痞坚、按之硬、结聚有形有点位
→
枳实
苦泄气锐如凿，对点破结。
气滞弥漫成“满”：腹满胀大、胸腹憋闷、面状无定点
→
厚朴
苦温宽缓如刨，大面积下气除满，兼降肺平喘。
临证要点
承气类中二药同用并非重复：厚朴先刨开弥漫之满、枳实再凿破聚结之痞，然后硝黄才能长驱直入——除满、破结、攻下是三道工序（后批承气类详展）。单见满用朴、单见结用枳，满结俱在则并投。
本组成员
枳实厚朴
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: the point and the plane of qi-descending herbs — the chisel that breaks binds vs the plane that removes fullness
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Qi stagnation congealed into a "bind": hard focal fullness below the heart, hard on pressure, gathered with tangible location
→
Zhi Shi
Bitter-draining, sharp in qi like a chisel, breaking a bind at the point.
Qi stagnation pervading into "fullness": abdominal distension, chest-abdomen oppression, spread out without a fixed point
→
Hou Po
Bitter-warm, broad and moderate like a plane, descending qi and removing fullness over a wide area, also descending the Lung and calming wheeze.
Clinical Key Points
In the Cheng Qi class the two used together is not redundancy: Hou Po first planes open the pervading fullness, Zhi Shi then chisels the gathered focal fullness, and only then can Mang Xiao-Da Huang drive straight in — removing fullness, breaking binds and purging are three operations. For fullness alone use Hou Po, for a bind alone use Zhi Shi, for both present use both.
Members of This Group
Zhi ShiHou Po
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

黄连 · 黄芩Huang Lian / Huang Qin

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：苦寒两兄弟的分工——心胃中焦之实火 vs 胆肺分野之郁热
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
火在心胃中焦：心下痞热、烦热欲呕、湿热下利之里急
→
黄连
大苦大寒质沉，直折其火、燥湿厚肠——对“实火湿热”下手。
热在胆肺分野：口苦往来寒热之热、肺热咳嗽、热利初起
→
黄芩
苦寒而轻于连，清泄郁于分野之热——柴胡剂之内翼。
临证要点
泻心诸方二药同用而黄连仅一两、黄芩三两，比例本身是病位声明：痞热弥漫于中上（芩之地界为主），心胃之火为辅。反之黄连汤连增至三两而去芩——火聚于胸、专折为务。读寒凉药先读剂量比，比清热二字信息量大得多。
本组成员
黄连黄芩
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: the division of the two bitter-cold brothers — repletion fire of the Heart-stomach middle burner vs constrained heat of the Gallbladder-Lung domain
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Fire in the Heart-stomach middle burner: focal-fullness heat below the heart, vexing heat with urge to vomit, damp-heat diarrhea with heaviness
→
Huang Lian
Greatly bitter and greatly cold, sinking-substanced, cutting the fire directly, drying damp and thickening the gut — striking at "repletion fire and damp-heat."
Heat in the Gallbladder-Lung domain: heat of bitter taste with alternating chills and fever, Lung-heat cough, early heat-diarrhea
→
Huang Qin
Bitter-cold but lighter than Huang Lian, clearing and draining heat constrained in the domain — the inner wing of the Chai Hu formulas.
Clinical Key Points
In the Xie Xin formulas the two are used together with Huang Lian at only one liang and Huang Qin at three — the ratio itself is a disease-location statement: focal-fullness heat pervading the middle and upper (Huang Qin's territory) is primary, Heart-stomach fire secondary. Conversely Huang Lian Tang raises Huang Lian to three liang and drops Huang Qin — fire gathered in the chest, focused cutting the task. To read cold-cooling herbs, first read the dose ratio; it carries far more than the words "clear heat."
Members of This Group
Huang LianHuang Qin
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

石膏 · 知母（附：栀子）Shi Gao / Zhi Mu / Zhi Zi

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：清气分热的三张面孔——辛寒清透 / 苦润清滋 / 轻清宣泄
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
热弥漫而盛、势仍外张（大热大汗脉洪）
→
石膏
辛甘大寒：清中带透，给热留外达之路，专折弥漫之焰。
热已劫津、燥象并见（大渴舌干）
→
知母
苦甘而润：清热兼滋其阴——石膏之最佳搭档，补其不能存津之短。
热郁于胸膈高位、无形无结（虚烦懊憹）
→
栀子
苦寒体轻：上清下导，郁火之专药，弥漫大热非其任。
临证要点
三药同属清气分，地界分明：栀子管“郁”、石膏管“盛”、知母管“燥”。郁热误投石膏则凉遏其宣（郁需透不需压），大热单用知母则力薄，津伤只予石膏则愈清愈燥——认准热的形态再选面孔。
本组成员
石膏知母栀子
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: the three faces of clearing qi-level heat — acrid-cold clearing-venting / bitter-moist clearing-enriching / light-clearing diffusing-draining
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Heat pervading and exuberant, still reaching outward (great heat, great sweat, surging pulse)
→
Shi Gao
Acrid-sweet and greatly cold: clearing with venting, leaving heat a route outward, cutting the pervading flame specifically.
Heat already plundering fluid, dryness signs present (great thirst, dry tongue)
→
Zhi Mu
Bitter-sweet and moist: clearing heat while enriching its yin — Shi Gao's best partner, mending its shortcoming of not preserving fluid.
Heat constrained at the high position of the chest-diaphragm, formless and unbound (deficiency-vexation anguish)
→
Zhi Zi
Bitter-cold and light-substanced: clearing above and conducting below, the specialist for constrained fire; pervading great heat is not its task.
Clinical Key Points
The three all clear the qi level but with distinct territories: Zhi Zi handles "constraint," Shi Gao handles "exuberance," Zhi Mu handles "dryness." Constrained heat mistakenly given Shi Gao suppresses its diffusion (constraint needs venting, not pressing); great heat given Zhi Mu alone is too weak; fluid-damage given only Shi Gao grows drier the more it clears — identify the heat's form, then choose the face.
Members of This Group
Shi GaoZhi MuZhi Zi
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

大黄 · 芒硝Da Huang / Mang Xiao

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：攻下二将的分工——推荡之力 vs 软坚之功
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
积滞在腑、须荡涤推出（并兼泻热行瘀诸用）
→
大黄
苦寒走泄如将军破阵：推陈致新，且煎法可调五种性格（后下锐、同煎缓、渍取气、酒洗行血、入丸缓图）。
燥屎坚结如石、推之不动
→
芒硝
咸寒软坚如水化冰：先软其坚，黄之推力方有着力处。
临证要点
大承气硝黄并用而小承气独黄无硝，界线即“坚”字：燥坚已成必配硝，痞满初结黄自足。调胃承气有硝黄而无朴枳，又示“燥热”与“痞满”两组证各有专药——四味药（黄硝朴枳）四个靶点，三承气是同一工具箱的三种取法。
本组成员
大黄芒硝
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: the division of the two purging generals — the force that flushes vs the skill that softens hardness
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Accumulation in the bowel, needing flushing out (also for draining heat and moving stasis)
→
Da Huang
Bitter-cold and draining like a general breaking an array: pushing out the stale to bring forth the new, and the decoction method can tune five characters (added last, sharp; boiled together, gentle; steeped, taking its qi; wine-washed, moving blood; made into a pill, gradual).
Dry stool bound hard as stone, immovable by pushing
→
Mang Xiao
Salty-cold softening hardness like water turning to ice: softening the hardness first, so Da Huang's pushing force has something to grip.
Clinical Key Points
Da Cheng Qi uses Mang Xiao and Da Huang together while Xiao Cheng Qi uses Da Huang alone without Mang Xiao — the boundary is the word "hardness": dry hardness formed must add Mang Xiao, early focal-fullness needs only Da Huang. Tiao Wei Cheng Qi has Mang Xiao-Da Huang but no Hou Po-Zhi Shi, showing again that the "dry-heat" and "focal-fullness" patterns each have their specialist herb — four herbs (Da Huang, Mang Xiao, Hou Po, Zhi Shi), four targets, the three Cheng Qi three ways of drawing from the same toolbox.
Members of This Group
Da HuangMang Xiao
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

茯苓 · 猪苓 · 泽泻Fu Ling / Zhu Ling / Ze Xie

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：利水三将的性格——守中兼渗 / 专职纯渗 / 深入而泄
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
水饮为患而兼心悸、脾虚（需利水且顾本）
→
茯苓
甘淡而平，利中带守：渗湿之外兼宁心健脾——利水药中唯一能当“君”挑大梁者（茯苓四逆、苓桂诸方）。
只需纯粹排水、不需兼职
→
猪苓
渗利之力最专最纯，无补无偏——阵中的纯排水工。
水蓄下焦深处、或水郁化热
→
泽泻
甘淡而寒，走得最深：直泄肾与膀胱之停水兼泄郁热——五苓以之为君即取其深。
临证要点
五苓、猪苓汤三将齐出并非堆砌：苓守其中、猪渗其中、泽泄其下，三个深度合围。单用时按需取性格：兼虚取苓、求纯取猪苓、位深或带热取泽泻。三将皆“渗”家——水已成形停蓄者用之；若水因阳虚而生生不息，渗利只是舀水，温阳（桂附）才是关阀。
本组成员
茯苓猪苓泽泻
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: the characters of the three water-draining generals — guarding the middle while percolating / purely percolating / reaching deep and draining
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Water-rheum trouble with palpitation and Spleen deficiency (needing to drain water while tending the root)
→
Fu Ling
Sweet-bland and even, benefiting with a measure of guarding: beyond percolating damp it also quiets the Heart and fortifies the Spleen — the only water-draining herb that can serve as sovereign (Fu Ling Si Ni, the Ling Gui formulas).
Needing pure water drainage alone, no second job
→
Zhu Ling
The most focused and pure in percolating-draining force, no supplement, no bias — the pure water-remover of the array.
Water amassed deep in the lower burner, or water constrained into heat
→
Ze Xie
Sweet-bland and cold, traveling deepest: draining lodged water straight from the Kidney and bladder and draining constrained heat — Wu Ling takes it as sovereign for its depth.
Clinical Key Points
Wu Ling and Zhu Ling Tang deploying all three is not piling up: Fu Ling guards the middle, Zhu Ling percolates the middle, Ze Xie drains below, three depths encircling. Used singly, choose by character: with deficiency take Fu Ling, for purity take Zhu Ling, for a deep position or accompanying heat take Ze Xie. All three are "percolators" — used when water has formed and lodged; if water arises endlessly from yang deficiency, percolating is only bailing, and warming yang (Gui-Fu) is closing the valve.
Members of This Group
Fu LingZhu LingZe Xie
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

吴茱萸 · 干姜 · 附子Wu Zhu Yu / Gan Jiang / Fu Zi

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：温里三将的地界——肝之脏寒 / 中焦脾胃 / 少阴命门
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
浊阴循肝经上逆：巅顶痛、呕涎沫、寒疝
→
吴茱萸
温里药中唯一主场在肝者——温其脏寒则浊阴自降。
中焦虚寒：吐利冷痛、食不化
→
干姜
守而不走，专温脾胃之轴。
阳衰于根：厥逆脉微、但欲寐
→
附子
走而不守，直入少阴起垂绝之阳。
临证要点
三将常联袂（附子理中、吴茱萸配姜）但各有不可替代之位：巅顶痛用姜附不应（不入肝经），命门火衰用吴萸不济（力不及根）。定温药先定寒之地界。
本组成员
吴茱萸干姜附子
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: the territories of the three warm-interior generals — the Liver's visceral cold / the Spleen-Stomach middle burner / Shao Yin Ming Men
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Turbid yin counterflowing up the Liver channel: vertex pain, vomiting of foam, cold-hernia
→
Wu Zhu Yu
The only warm-interior herb with its main field in the Liver — warming its visceral cold and the turbid yin descends of itself.
Middle-burner deficiency-cold: vomiting-diarrhea with cold pain, undigested food
→
Gan Jiang
Guards and does not travel, warming the Spleen-Stomach axle specifically.
Yang debilitated at the root: reversal with a faint pulse, desire only to sleep
→
Fu Zi
Travels and does not guard, entering Shao Yin directly to raise the about-to-expire yang.
Clinical Key Points
The three often go together (Fu Zi Li Zhong, Wu Zhu Yu with ginger) but each has an irreplaceable position: vertex pain given ginger-Fu Zi does not answer (they do not enter the Liver channel), Ming Men fire debilitation given Wu Zhu Yu does not help (its force does not reach the root). To fix a warm herb, first fix the cold's territory.
Members of This Group
Wu Zhu YuGan JiangFu Zi
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

当归 · 川芎 · 芍药Dang Gui / Chuan Xiong / Shao Yao

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：血家三味的分工——补而行 / 行而散 / 敛而柔
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
血虚为主、兼需温通（脉细、肢冷、经少色淡）
→
当归
补血而性动，血中圣药——虚而滞者最宜。
血滞不行、郁而作痛（刺痛、头痛、经行不畅）
→
川芎
血中气药，纯行无补——静药阵中的发动机。
血不柔筋、拘急挛痛（腹痛、脚挛急）
→
芍药
酸敛养营，柔肝缓急——血家的刹车与润滑。
临证要点
三味同出（当归芍药散、温经汤、后世四物）不是堆料：归补、芎动、芍敛，三个方向互相制衡——芎无归芍则燥散伤血，归芍无芎则壅补滞血。剂量比即方向盘：芍重则主缓急（当归芍药散芍一斤），芎重则主行滞。
本组成员
当归川芎芍药
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: the division of the three blood-family herbs — supplementing yet moving / moving yet dispersing / gathering yet softening
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Blood deficiency predominant, also needing warm-freeing (thin pulse, cold limbs, scant pale menses)
→
Dang Gui
Supplementing blood with a moving nature, the sage-herb within the blood — most apt for deficiency with stagnation.
Blood stagnation not moving, constrained into pain (stabbing pain, headache, uneven menses)
→
Chuan Xiong
The qi-herb within blood, purely moving without supplementing — the engine in a board of still herbs.
Blood not softening the sinews, cramping pain (abdominal pain, cramping legs)
→
Shao Yao
Sour-gathering and nourishing the nutritive, softening the Liver and relieving urgency — the blood family's brake and lubricant.
Clinical Key Points
The three appearing together (Dang Gui Shao Yao San, Wen Jing Tang, the later Si Wu) is not piling material: Dang Gui supplements, Chuan Xiong moves, Shao Yao gathers, three directions checking one another — Chuan Xiong without Dang Gui-Shao Yao dries, disperses and harms blood; Dang Gui-Shao Yao without Chuan Xiong congest, supplement and stagnate blood. The dose ratio is the steering wheel: Shao Yao heavy governs relieving urgency (Dang Gui Shao Yao San's one jin of Shao Yao), Chuan Xiong heavy governs moving stagnation.
Members of This Group
Dang GuiChuan XiongShao Yao
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

补气药选用Qi-Tonics: Which One

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：同为补气，走向与兼能各异——救脱固里选人参、走表固卫升举选黄芪、平补清润守而不燥选党参、燥湿健脾选白术、补涩兼固选山药、和中缓急选甘草；“气虚”二字之下，先问兼夹（脱？表虚？津伤？湿？滑脱？）再落子
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
气虚欲脱：大汗、脉微欲绝、气短息弱
→
人参
大补元气救里、生津固脱——独参汤救急之本；力峻价高，虚脱重证方显其必要，非日常平补所需。
一般气虚（脾肺气弱、食少倦怠气短）、日常平补、气津两伤
→
党参
人参的日常替身：平补脾肺兼生津养血、性平可久服——但无救脱之力，虚脱急证仍须人参，此即替代之边界。
气虚兼表虚自汗、易感冒、清阳下陷（脏器下垂久泻脱肛）
→
黄芪
补气而性升走表：固卫止汗（玉屏风）、升举下陷（补中益气）、行水通痹——参守里而芪走表升，一味定“方向”。
气虚兼脾湿：食少便溏、苔白、肢困
→
白术
补气之中兼燥湿健运——四君之臣、气虚夹湿之的；纯气虚无湿者不必其燥。
气虚兼阴伤、或需长服平补（脾肺气阴两弱）
→
山药
甘平补涩同体、补而不燥不峻——久服可任（薯蓣丸治虚劳），兼固精止泻；救急不足、缓图有余，与人参恰成急缓两端。
气虚而但需和中缓急、调和诸药
→
甘草
补气力最缓而调和之职最广——非主补之帅，乃全方之“和”：缓急、解毒、协诸药，用量过则壅满。
临证要点
第一陷阱是“见气虚就黄芪”：黄芪之长在走表升举，气虚欲脱当用人参（救里固脱）、气阴两虚当参麦（芪之温升反耗阴）、纯里虚无下陷者芪之升性用不上。第二是“党参代人参”的边界——党参平补清润、无救脱之力，虚脱急证以党参代参等于杯水车薪：党参平补清润、无救脱之力，日常气虚党参足矣、救急独参不可代——较人参平缓、较山药专于补气而兼生津。第三是“补而不行”：大队补气药需一味陈皮/木香“静中一动”防壅，纯补则滞。第四是黄芪与党参的教学分野——芪偏“走与升”（卫表、下陷、水气）、党参偏“守与润”（脾肺、津亏、平补），同治气虚而选择全看兼夹方向，不看“补气”二字本身。
本组成员
人参党参黄芪白术山药甘草
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: all supplement qi, but with different directions and secondary abilities — for rescuing desertion and consolidating the interior choose ginseng; for traveling the exterior, consolidating the defensive and raising up choose Huang Qi; for even supplementation, clearing-moistening and guarding without drying choose Dang Shen; for drying damp and fortifying the Spleen choose Bai Zhu; for supplement-and-astringe choose Shan Yao; for harmonizing the middle and relieving urgency choose Gan Cao. Under the words "qi deficiency," first ask the accompaniment (desertion? exterior deficiency? fluid damage? damp? slippage?) then place the piece.
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Qi deficiency verging on desertion: great sweat, faint pulse verging on expiry, shortness of breath
→
Ren Shen
Greatly supplementing original qi to rescue the interior, engendering fluid and consolidating desertion — the root of Du Shen Tang for acute rescue; fierce and costly, its necessity shown in severe desertion, not needed for everyday even supplementation.
Ordinary qi deficiency (Spleen-Lung weakness, reduced appetite, fatigue), everyday even supplementation, qi and fluid both harmed
→
Dang Shen
Ginseng's everyday stand-in: evenly supplementing Spleen-Lung while engendering fluid and nourishing blood, even in nature and bearing long use — but without desertion-rescuing power, acute desertion still requiring ginseng, this being the boundary of substitution.
Qi deficiency with exterior-deficiency spontaneous sweat, easy contraction, clear-yang sinking (organ prolapse, chronic diarrhea, rectal prolapse)
→
Huang Qi
Supplementing qi with a raising nature traveling the exterior: consolidating the defensive and stopping sweat (Yu Ping Feng), raising the sunken (Bu Zhong Yi Qi), moving water and freeing bi — ginseng guards the interior and Huang Qi travels the exterior and raises, one herb setting the "direction."
Qi deficiency with Spleen damp: reduced appetite, loose stool, white coat, heavy limbs
→
Bai Zhu
Supplementing qi while drying damp and fortifying transport — the minister of Si Jun, the target of qi-deficiency with damp; pure qi deficiency without damp need not use its drying.
Qi deficiency with yin damage, or needing long-term even supplementation (Spleen-Lung qi-and-yin both weak)
→
Shan Yao
Sweet-even, supplement-and-astringe in one body, supplementing without drying or fierceness — bearing long use (Shu Yu Wan treats taxation), also consolidating essence and stopping diarrhea; insufficient for acute rescue, ample for gradual work, forming with ginseng the two ends of urgent and gradual.
Qi deficiency needing only to harmonize the middle, relieve urgency and harmonize the herbs
→
Gan Cao
The most moderate in qi-supplementing force but broadest in the harmonizing office — not a supplementing marshal but the "harmony" of the whole formula: relieving urgency, resolving toxin, coordinating the herbs; overdosed it congests and fills.
Clinical Key Points
The first trap is "qi deficiency means Huang Qi": Huang Qi's strength is traveling the exterior and raising up; qi deficiency verging on desertion needs ginseng (rescuing the interior, consolidating desertion), qi-and-yin dual deficiency needs ginseng-Mai Dong (Huang Qi's warm-raising conversely consumes yin), pure interior deficiency without sinking has no use for Huang Qi's raising. The second is the boundary of "Dang Shen substituting for ginseng" — Dang Shen evenly supplements and clear-moistens with no desertion-rescuing power; in acute desertion Dang Shen for ginseng is a cup of water on a burning cart: for everyday qi deficiency Dang Shen suffices, for acute rescue ginseng alone is irreplaceable. The third is "supplement without moving": a board of qi-supplementing herbs needs one Chen Pi or Mu Xiang as "one mover amid stillness" to prevent congestion. The fourth is the teaching division of Huang Qi and Dang Shen — Huang Qi biased to "traveling and raising" (defensive exterior, sinking, water qi), Dang Shen biased to "guarding and moistening" (Spleen-Lung, fluid depletion, even supplementation); both treat qi deficiency and the choice depends entirely on the accompaniment's direction, not the words "supplement qi."
Members of This Group
Ren ShenDang ShenHuang QiBai ZhuShan YaoGan Cao
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

补血药选用Blood-Tonics: Which One

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：补血四主将各有偏性——熟地厚重填精血之本、当归补中有行兼调经、白芍敛阴柔肝止痛、阿胶滋阴兼止血；“血虚”之下先问：偏静补（地芍胶）还是需兼行（归）、有无出血（胶）、有无肝急疼痛（芍）
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
血虚之本、精血亏（腰膝酸、须发早白、经少）
→
地黄
熟地厚味填精、补血之最——但滋腻碍胃，便溏者伍砂仁或减量。
血虚兼血滞、需补而兼行（月经不调、血虚而痛）
→
当归
补血中之动药：补而能行、调经止痛——四物之“动”，与地芍之静互补。
血虚兼肝急、筋脉拘挛疼痛、阴虚阳浮
→
芍药
白芍敛阴柔肝、缓急止痛——酸收之性，芍药甘草之核；补血之外独任“柔”与“敛”。
血虚兼出血、或阴虚（崩漏、咳血、心烦不眠）
→
阿胶
血肉有情，滋阴补血兼止血——黄连阿胶之滋、胶艾之止，补血药中独兼止血一职。
临证要点
陷阱在“补血不辨动静”：纯静补（地芍胶）用于血虚兼瘀则愈滞，需加当归之行或径入活血；当归之温香用于阴虚火旺则助热。熟地之腻、阿胶之滞皆碍脾，脾虚便溏者补血须兼运中（砂仁陈皮）或改用平补（何首乌、桑椹）。白芍之酸敛用于虚寒里急者当配桂（小建中）而非独用。
本组成员
地黄当归芍药阿胶
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: the four blood-supplementing generals each with a bias — Shu Di thick and heavy filling the root of essence-blood; Dang Gui supplementing with movement and regulating menses; Bai Shao gathering yin, softening the Liver and stopping pain; E Jiao enriching yin and also stopping bleeding. Under "blood deficiency" first ask: still supplementation (Di-Shao-E Jiao) or needing movement too (Dang Gui), any bleeding (E Jiao), any Liver-urgency pain (Shao Yao).
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
The root of blood deficiency, essence-blood depletion (aching lower back and knees, premature graying, scant menses)
→
Di Huang
Shu Di thick-flavored filling essence, the foremost blood supplement — but cloying and hindering the stomach, paired with Sha Ren or reduced with loose stool.
Blood deficiency with stasis, needing supplement plus movement (irregular menses, blood deficiency with pain)
→
Dang Gui
The mover within blood supplements: supplementing yet able to move, regulating menses and stopping pain — the "movement" of Si Wu, complementing the stillness of Di-Shao.
Blood deficiency with Liver urgency, cramping sinew pain, yin deficiency with yang floating
→
Shao Yao
Bai Shao gathers yin and softens the Liver, relieving urgency and stopping pain — the sour-gathering nature, the core of Shao Yao Gan Cao; beyond supplementing blood it alone takes "softening" and "gathering."
Blood deficiency with bleeding, or yin deficiency (flooding, coughing blood, vexation sleeplessness)
→
E Jiao
A blood-flesh substance, enriching yin and supplementing blood while stopping bleeding — the enriching of Huang Lian E Jiao, the stopping of Jiao Ai, the only blood-supplement also holding the bleeding-stopping office.
Clinical Key Points
The trap is "supplementing blood without distinguishing movement and stillness": pure still supplementation (Di-Shao-E Jiao) used for blood deficiency with stasis grows more stagnant, needing Dang Gui's movement added or a blood-invigorator directly; Dang Gui's warm aroma used for yin-deficiency fire hyperactivity abets heat. Shu Di's cloying and E Jiao's stagnation both hinder the Spleen, so with Spleen-deficiency loose stool, blood supplementation must also transport the middle (Sha Ren, Chen Pi) or switch to even supplementation (He Shou Wu, mulberry). Bai Shao's sour-gathering for deficiency-cold interior urgency should be paired with Gui (Xiao Jian Zhong), not used alone.
Members of This Group
Di HuangDang GuiShao YaoE Jiao
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

活血药选用Blood-Movers: Which One

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：活血按“力度与兼能”排谱——丹参凉血活血兼安神（缓）、桃仁红花逐瘀通经（中·破有形血块用桃、行血滞用红）、川芎血中气药兼行气祛风（上行头目）、五灵脂蒲黄专止瘀痛、益母草兼利水调经；先问：瘀之新久缓急？兼热（丹）兼气滞（芎）兼水（益母）兼痛（灵脂蒲黄）？
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
瘀而兼热、或需缓行久服（胸痹心悸、瘀热证）
→
丹参
凉血活血、性平缓不伤正兼安神——“一味丹参功同四物”，久服可任，与桃红之峻攻分档。
瘀血有形之块、经闭癥瘕（较峻）
→
桃仁
质重润降、破有形之瘀块——与红花成对：桃破块、红行滞。
血行不畅之滞、通经活血（较轻扬）
→
红花
辛散轻扬、行血中之滞——少用养血、多用破血，逐瘀方通用药组。
瘀兼气滞、头目之疾（头痛、经行不畅）
→
川芎
血中气药：行气活血、上行头目祛风——瘀而气不行者非它不可，与香附气血镜像。
瘀血作痛为主（心腹刺痛、经痛有块）
→
五灵脂
化瘀止痛之专才、与蒲黄成失笑之对——痛甚者领衔；畏人参（十九畏）。
瘀而兼出血、或产后恶露（瘀痛兼漏）
→
蒲黄
生用行血、炒用止血——一药两向，瘀血兼出血不忌。
妇科瘀滞兼水肿（经产、水瘀互见）
→
益母草
活血调经兼利水——血水同治之身，经产要药。
临证要点
活血药孕妇多忌（桃红益母灵脂尤甚）、出血倾向者慎。第一陷阱是不辨缓峻：轻证久服用丹参、急证攻坚用桃红逐瘀，误则或不及或伤正。第二是“活血不理气”：气行则血行，纯活血不佐行气（芎香）则力减——血府逐瘀之配四逆散即此理。第三是灵脂人参相畏，同用须知十九畏。
本组成员
丹参桃仁红花川芎五灵脂蒲黄益母草
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: blood-invigorators arrayed by "strength and secondary ability" — Dan Shen cools blood and invigorates while calming the spirit (gentle); Tao Ren-Hong Hua expel stasis and free menses (moderate; Tao Ren breaks tangible clots, Hong Hua moves blood stagnation); Chuan Xiong the qi-herb within blood also moving qi and expelling wind (ascending to the head); Wu Ling Zhi-Pu Huang stop stasis pain specifically; Yi Mu Cao also benefits water and regulates menses. First ask: the stasis new or old, gentle or urgent? With heat (Dan Shen), qi stagnation (Chuan Xiong), water (Yi Mu), pain (Wu Ling Zhi-Pu Huang)?
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Stasis with heat, or needing gentle movement for long use (chest bi, palpitation, stasis-heat)
→
Dan Shen
Cooling blood and invigorating, even and gentle in nature without harming the upright, also calming the spirit — "a single Dan Shen has the merit of Si Wu," bearing long use, a gradation from the fierce attack of Tao-Hong.
Tangible stasis clots, blocked menses and concretions (more fierce)
→
Tao Ren
Heavy-substanced, moist-descending, breaking tangible stasis clots — paired with Hong Hua: Tao Ren breaks clots, Hong Hua moves stagnation.
Stagnation of uneven blood flow, freeing menses and invigorating blood (lighter and rising)
→
Hong Hua
Acrid-dispersing and light-rising, moving the stagnation within blood — small dose nourishes blood, large dose breaks it, the general blood-moving set of stasis-expelling formulas.
Stasis with qi stagnation, head-and-eye ailments (headache, uneven menses)
→
Chuan Xiong
The qi-herb within blood: moving qi and invigorating blood, ascending to the head and eyes and expelling wind — for stasis with qi not moving nothing else will do, a qi-blood mirror to Xiang Fu.
Stasis pain predominant (stabbing heart-abdomen pain, menstrual pain with clots)
→
Wu Ling Zhi
A specialist in transforming stasis and stopping pain, paired with Pu Huang as Shi Xiao — leading for severe pain; fears ginseng (the nineteen antagonisms).
Stasis with bleeding, or postpartum lochia (stasis pain with leakage)
→
Pu Huang
Raw it moves blood, fried it stops bleeding — one herb two directions, not shunned in stasis with bleeding.
Gynecological stasis with edema (menstruation-delivery, water and stasis together)
→
Yi Mu Cao
Invigorating blood and regulating menses while benefiting water — a blood-water co-treating body, an essential for menstruation and delivery.
Clinical Key Points
Blood-invigorators are mostly shunned in pregnancy (Tao-Hong, Yi Mu, Wu Ling Zhi especially) and used with caution in bleeding tendency. The first trap is not distinguishing gentle and fierce: light chronic cases and long use take Dan Shen, acute hardness-attacking takes Tao-Hong stasis-expelling; misjudged, it either falls short or harms the upright. The second is "invigorating blood without regulating qi": qi moving, blood moves, and pure blood-invigoration without a qi-mover (Chuan Xiong, Xiang) loses force — Xue Fu Zhu Yu combining with Si Ni San is this principle. The third is Wu Ling Zhi and ginseng in mutual fear; using them together requires knowing the nineteen antagonisms.
Members of This Group
Dan ShenTao RenHong HuaChuan XiongWu Ling ZhiPu HuangYi Mu Cao
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

安神药选用Shen-Calmers: Which One

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：安神三路——养（枣仁柏仁补血养心之“虚烦”）、通（远志交通心肾之“不接”）、镇（龙骨牡蛎龙齿潜镇浮阳之“惊越”）；先问不寐之本：血不养神？心肾不交？阳浮神动？
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
肝血虚之虚烦不眠、心悸多梦（劳心过度）
→
酸枣仁
养肝血安魂——酸枣仁汤之君，血不养神之的；与柏仁分工：枣仁入肝、柏仁入心。
心血虚之失眠兼便干、心神不宁
→
柏子仁
养心安神兼润肠——质润多脂，虚烦兼肠燥一举两得。
心肾不交之失眠健忘、或痰阻心窍
→
远志
交通心肾（安神三路之“通”）、兼祛痰开窍——养镇不效而属“心肾不接”者，通之即效。
阳浮神越之惊悸怔忡、烦躁失眠（兼滑脱）
→
龙骨
重镇潜阳安神兼收敛固涩——桂甘龙牡之敛，与牡蛎成潜镇之对。
阴虚阳亢之惊悸、兼痰核瘰疬（软坚）
→
牡蛎
潜阳安神兼软坚散结——较龙骨多“软坚”一职（消瘰、镇肝熄风皆用）。
惊悸夜惊、魂梦不安（镇惊尤专）
→
龙齿
龙齿镇惊之力专于龙骨——安神定志丸之镇，气虚胆怯之惊的选。
临证要点
安神先辨虚实：虚者（血虚阴虚）养与滋、实者（痰火）当清（温胆黄连），纯用安神药于痰火不寐则苔腻愈壅。三路各有其证——养药（枣柏）治“血不养”、通药（远志）治“心肾不接”、镇药（龙牡）治“阳浮神越”，错路则无功。矿石镇药（龙牡龙齿）质重碍胃，脾虚者不宜久服、宜先煎。
本组成员
酸枣仁柏子仁远志龙骨牡蛎龙齿
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: three lines of spirit-calming — nourish (Suan Zao Ren, Bai Zi Ren supplementing blood and nourishing the Heart for "deficiency-vexation"), free (Yuan Zhi linking Heart and Kidney for "non-connection"), subdue (Long Gu, Mu Li, Long Chi subduing floating yang for "fright-desertion"). First ask the root of sleeplessness: blood not nourishing the spirit? Heart and Kidney not interacting? Yang floating and the spirit stirring?
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Liver-blood-deficiency vexing sleeplessness, palpitation with many dreams (over-taxed heart)
→
Suan Zao Ren
Nourishing Liver blood and calming the ethereal soul — the sovereign of Suan Zao Ren Tang, the target of blood not nourishing the spirit; divided from Bai Zi Ren: Suan Zao Ren enters the Liver, Bai Zi Ren the Heart.
Heart-blood-deficiency insomnia with dry stool, restless spirit
→
Bai Zi Ren
Nourishing the Heart and calming the spirit while moistening the bowel — moist and oily, hitting two birds in deficiency-vexation with dry bowel.
Heart-Kidney non-interaction insomnia and forgetfulness, or phlegm obstructing the Heart orifices
→
Yuan Zhi
Linking Heart and Kidney (the "free" line of spirit-calming), also expelling phlegm and opening the orifices — where nourishing and subduing fail and it is "Heart-Kidney non-connection," freeing it takes effect.
Yang-floating spirit-deserting fright-palpitation, vexation sleeplessness (with slippage)
→
Long Gu
Heavily subduing yang and calming the spirit while gathering and astringing — the gathering of Gui Gan Long Mu, forming with Mu Li a subduing pair.
Yin-deficiency yang-hyperactivity fright-palpitation, with phlegm-nodes and scrofula (softening hardness)
→
Mu Li
Subduing yang and calming the spirit while softening hardness and dissipating binds — one "softening" office more than Long Gu (used in Xiao Luo, Zhen Gan Xi Feng).
Fright-palpitation, night terrors, restless dreams (subduing fright especially focused)
→
Long Chi
Long Chi's fright-subduing force is more focused than Long Gu's — the subduing of An Shen Ding Zhi Wan, the choice for fright of qi-deficiency timidity.
Clinical Key Points
Calming the spirit first distinguishes deficiency and repletion: the deficient (blood-deficiency, yin-deficiency) nourish and enrich, the replete (phlegm-fire) clear (Wen Dan, Huang Lian); pure spirit-calmers for phlegm-fire sleeplessness make the greasy coat more congested. Each line has its pattern — nourishers (Zao-Bai) treat "blood not nourishing," the freer (Yuan Zhi) treats "Heart-Kidney non-connection," subduers (Long-Mu) treat "yang floating and spirit deserting"; the wrong line has no effect. Mineral subduers (Long-Mu, Long Chi) are heavy and hinder the stomach, unfit for long use in the Spleen-deficient and best boiled first.
Members of This Group
Suan Zao RenBai Zi RenYuan ZhiLong GuMu LiLong Chi
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

理气药选用Qi-Movers: Which One

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：理气按“部位与力度”分工——陈皮中焦脾胃兼化痰（平）、木香脾肠之滞除后重（平）、香附肝郁调经之总司（平·女科）、枳实破气消痞（峻·实证）、厚朴行气除满兼降逆（下气）、乌药下焦寒凝、砂仁芳香化湿醒脾；先问：气滞在何部（中/肝/肠/下焦）？虚证宜芳缓、实证宜破降
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
脾胃气滞兼痰湿（脘胀、痰多、食少）
→
橘皮
理气健脾兼化痰——最平和通用、补剂防壅之常任（“治痰先治气”）。
脾肠气滞、泻痢后重、脘腹胀痛
→
木香
行脾肠之滞、除后重——芍药汤之气翼、虚人行滞之首（“静中一动”）。
肝郁气滞、胁痛、月经不调乳胀
→
香附
疏肝之总司、女科之主帅——性平不偏寒热，肝郁无论兼寒兼热皆可帅。
气滞实证之痞满便结（较峻）
→
枳实
破气消痞、力猛下行——承气之凿、枳术之破；虚人慎（易伤正），枳壳缓于枳实。
气滞兼湿满、或胃气上逆（胀满、喘咳）
→
厚朴
行气除满兼燥湿降逆——“满”之专药（平胃、承气、半夏厚朴皆用）。
下焦寒凝气滞（少腹痛、疝、尿频）
→
乌药
行气药中独走下焦、兼温肾散寒——寒凝下焦之的（缩泉、膈下逐瘀）。
湿阻气滞、脾胃呆钝（脘痞纳呆、补药滋腻）
→
砂仁
芳香化湿醒脾——“熟地伍砂仁”化滋腻之滞，湿滞与滋补两阵通用。
临证要点
理气药多辛温香燥，易耗气伤阴：气虚者行气须兼补（香砂六君）、阴虚者慎香燥（一贯煎宁用川楝之凉）。第一陷阱是虚实不分：实证之胀用枳朴破降、虚证之滞用陈木香芳行，虚证误破则愈虚。第二是部位错配：肝郁不用香附而用陈皮则不入肝、下焦寒凝不用乌药而用陈皮则不达下。行气过用则耗气，中病即止。
本组成员
橘皮木香香附枳实厚朴乌药砂仁
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: qi-movers divided by "location and strength" — Chen Pi the Spleen-Stomach middle burner also transforming phlegm (even); Mu Xiang the Spleen-intestine stagnation removing heaviness (even); Xiang Fu the commander of Liver-constraint and menstrual regulation (even, gynecology); Zhi Shi breaking qi and dissolving focal fullness (fierce, repletion); Hou Po moving qi, removing fullness and descending counterflow; Wu Yao the lower-burner cold-congelation; Sha Ren aromatic, transforming damp and rousing the Spleen. First ask: where is the qi stagnation (middle / Liver / intestine / lower burner)? Deficiency patterns suit aromatic-moderate, repletion patterns suit breaking-descending.
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Spleen-Stomach qi stagnation with phlegm-damp (epigastric distension, much phlegm, reduced appetite)
→
Ju Pi
Regulating qi and fortifying the Spleen while transforming phlegm — the most even and versatile, a standing part of supplementing formulas to prevent congestion ("to treat phlegm first treat qi").
Spleen-intestine qi stagnation, diarrhea-dysentery with heaviness below, epigastric-abdominal distending pain
→
Mu Xiang
Moving Spleen-intestine stagnation and removing heaviness — the qi wing of Shao Yao Tang, the first choice for moving stagnation in the deficient ("one mover amid stillness").
Liver-constraint qi stagnation, hypochondriac pain, irregular menses, breast distension
→
Xiang Fu
The commander of Liver-coursing, the marshal of gynecology — even in nature without cold-heat bias, able to marshal Liver-constraint with cold or heat alike.
Repletion-pattern qi stagnation with focal fullness and bound stool (more fierce)
→
Zhi Shi
Breaking qi and dissolving focal fullness, fierce and downward — the chisel of Cheng Qi, the breaking of Zhi Zhu; used with caution in the deficient (easily harms the upright), Zhi Ke gentler than Zhi Shi.
Qi stagnation with damp-fullness, or stomach-qi counterflow (distension, wheeze-cough)
→
Hou Po
Moving qi and removing fullness while drying damp and descending counterflow — the specialist for "fullness" (used in Ping Wei, Cheng Qi, Ban Xia Hou Po).
Lower-burner cold-congelation qi stagnation (lower-abdomen pain, hernia, urinary frequency)
→
Wu Yao
The only qi-mover traveling the lower burner alone, also warming the Kidney and dispersing cold — the target of lower-burner cold-congelation (Suo Quan, Ge Xia Zhu Yu).
Damp-obstruction qi stagnation, dull Spleen-Stomach (epigastric fullness, poor appetite, cloying supplements)
→
Sha Ren
Aromatic, transforming damp and rousing the Spleen — "Shu Di paired with Sha Ren" transforms cloying stagnation, common to both damp-stagnation and enriching-supplement arrays.
Clinical Key Points
Qi-movers are mostly acrid-warm, aromatic and drying, easily consuming qi and harming yin: in qi deficiency, moving qi must accompany supplementation (Xiang Sha Liu Jun); in yin deficiency, use aromatic-drying with caution (Yi Guan Jian prefers the cooling Chuan Lian Zi). The first trap is not distinguishing deficiency and repletion: repletion distension uses the breaking-descending Zhi Shi-Hou Po, deficiency stagnation uses the aromatic-moving Chen Pi-Mu Xiang; breaking a deficiency pattern makes it more deficient. The second is location mismatch: Liver-constraint not using Xiang Fu but Chen Pi does not enter the Liver, lower-burner cold-congelation not using Wu Yao but Chen Pi does not reach below. Overused, qi-moving consumes qi — stop once it hits the mark.
Members of This Group
Ju PiMu XiangXiang FuZhi ShiHou PoWu YaoSha Ren
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

化痰药选用Phlegm-Resolvers: Which One

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：化痰按痰之“寒热燥湿”分路——半夏燥湿痰之君（温燥）、贝母润燥热痰兼散结（凉润·川润浙泄）、竹茹清痰热除烦（凉）、瓜蒌润肺涤痰宽胸（甘寒·痰热结胸）、白芥子豁皮里膜外之痰（温·经络关节）；先辨痰性：白滑湿痰（夏）、黄黏热痰（茹贝蒌）、干黏燥痰（贝）、深伏膜外（芥）
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
湿痰白滑、量多易咯、苔白腻、呕恶
→
半夏
燥湿化痰之君、降逆和胃——二陈之核，湿痰通用；阴虚燥痰忌其燥。
燥痰热痰之干咳黏痰、或瘰疬痰核
→
贝母
润化燥热之痰兼散结——与半夏成燥润两仪；川贝润虚劳、浙贝泄风热散核。
痰热之烦呕、惊悸不眠（胆胃）
→
竹茹
清痰热、除烦止呕——温胆之核，与半夏一温一凉相制。
痰热结胸、胸痛便秘、痰黄稠
→
栝楼实
清热涤痰、宽胸散结兼润下——小陷胸之君，痰热互结之的。
痰在皮里膜外、经络关节、阴疽流注
→
白芥子
辛温豁“皮里膜外之痰”——他药不达之处芥子达之（三子养亲、阳和汤）。
临证要点
化痰第一辨燥湿寒热：湿痰用夏（燥）、燥痰用贝（润），误则燥者愈燥、湿者愈黏。第二是治痰治源——痰源在脾，攻痰不健脾（茯苓白术）则痰去复生。半夏贝母同反乌头（十八反），瓜蒌亦反乌头，痰药三将同一配伍禁。阴虚燥咳忌温燥（半夏白芥），痰热忌温补。
本组成员
半夏贝母竹茹栝楼实白芥子
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: phlegm-transformers divided by the phlegm's "cold-heat-dry-damp" — Ban Xia the sovereign of damp phlegm (warm-drying); Bei Mu moistening dry and heat phlegm and dissipating binds (cool-moist; Chuan moistens, Zhe drains); Zhu Ru clearing phlegm-heat and removing vexation (cool); Gua Lou moistening the Lung, sweeping phlegm and widening the chest (sweet-cold, phlegm-heat chest-bind); Bai Jie Zi opening phlegm beneath the membranes (warm, channels and joints). First distinguish the phlegm's nature: white slippery damp phlegm (Ban Xia), yellow sticky heat phlegm (Zhu Ru, Bei Mu, Gua Lou), dry sticky dry phlegm (Bei Mu), deeply lodged beyond the membranes (Bai Jie Zi).
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Damp phlegm white and slippery, copious and easy to expectorate, white greasy coat, nausea
→
Ban Xia
The sovereign of drying damp and transforming phlegm, descending counterflow and harmonizing the stomach — the core of Er Chen, versatile for damp phlegm; yin-deficiency dry phlegm shuns its drying.
Dry cough with sticky phlegm of dry or heat phlegm, or scrofula and phlegm-nodes
→
Bei Mu
Moist-transforming dry and heat phlegm while dissipating binds — the drying-moistening two principles with Ban Xia; Chuan Bei moistens taxation, Zhe Bei drains wind-heat and dissolves nodes.
Phlegm-heat vexation-retching, fright-palpitation sleeplessness (Gallbladder-stomach)
→
Zhu Ru
Clearing phlegm-heat, removing vexation and stopping retching — the core of Wen Dan, one warm and one cool checking Ban Xia.
Phlegm-heat chest-bind, chest pain with constipation, yellow thick phlegm
→
Gua Lou Shi
Clearing heat, sweeping phlegm, widening the chest and dissipating binds while moistening downward — the sovereign of Xiao Xian Xiong, the target of phlegm-heat binding.
Phlegm beneath the skin and membranes, in channels and joints, yin-abscess flowing
→
Bai Jie Zi
Acrid-warm, opening "phlegm beneath the membranes" — where other herbs cannot reach, Bai Jie Zi reaches (San Zi Yang Qin, Yang He Tang).
Clinical Key Points
Transforming phlegm first distinguishes dry-damp and cold-heat: damp phlegm uses Ban Xia (drying), dry phlegm uses Bei Mu (moistening); misjudged, the dry grows drier and the damp stickier. The second is treating phlegm at its source — the phlegm source is in the Spleen, and attacking phlegm without fortifying the Spleen (Fu Ling, Bai Zhu) lets phlegm regenerate. Ban Xia and Bei Mu both oppose Wu Tou (the eighteen incompatibilities), and Gua Lou too opposes Wu Tou, the three phlegm generals under one combination prohibition. Yin-deficiency dry cough shuns warm-drying (Ban Xia, Bai Jie Zi), phlegm-heat shuns warm supplementation.
Members of This Group
Ban XiaBei MuZhu RuGua Lou ShiBai Jie Zi
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

利水药选用Water-Drainers: Which One

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：利水按“兼能”分工——茯苓利水兼健脾宁心（平·补中有利）、猪苓泽泻纯利力专（泽兼泄肾火）、滑石利窍兼清暑（寒）、薏苡仁渗湿兼健脾除痹排脓（缓·可久）、车前子利水兼清肝明目、防己行肌表经隧之水兼祛风湿；先问：利水兼补（苓）、纯利（猪泽）、兼热（滑车）、兼湿痹（薏己）
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
水湿内停兼脾虚、心悸失眠（补中有利）
→
茯苓
利水渗湿兼健脾宁心——平和可久、补剂常伍（苓桂、四君皆在），利水药中唯一“补”性者。
水湿壅盛、需纯利力专（水肿、小便不利）
→
猪苓
纯利渗湿、力专于苓而无补性——猪苓汤之君，实证利水。
水湿兼下焦虚火、或痰饮眩冒
→
泽泻
利水兼泄肾火——泽泻汤治冒眩、六味三泻之一，利中带清。
湿热淋痛、暑热烦渴溲赤
→
滑石
滑利窍道兼清暑——八正之利、六一之君，湿热之的。
脾虚湿盛兼筋脉拘挛、或痈脓（缓补可久）
→
薏苡仁
渗湿健脾兼除痹排脓——力缓性和可久服，经方时方通吃。
水肿兼肝热目赤、或湿热泻
→
车前子
利水通淋兼清肝明目、利小便实大便——湿热与肝热并见者宜。
肌表经隧之水（风水身重、湿热痹关节肿）
→
防己
行肌表经隧之水、兼祛风湿——与苓泽走水道分疆，湿热痹之的。
临证要点
利水药多渗利伤阴，阴虚津亏者慎（猪苓汤故加阿胶护阴）。第一陷阱是“利水不辨补泻”：脾虚水停宜茯苓（补中利）、实证壅盛宜猪泽（纯利），虚证纯利则愈虚。第二是寒热——湿热用滑车（寒）、寒湿用苓桂（温），误则寒者凝、热者炽。中病即止，勿过利伤津。
本组成员
茯苓猪苓泽泻滑石薏苡仁车前子防己
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: water-drainers divided by "secondary ability" — Fu Ling draining water while fortifying the Spleen and quieting the Heart (even, benefiting within supplementing); Zhu Ling-Ze Xie purely and focusedly draining (Ze Xie also drains Kidney fire); Hua Shi benefiting the orifices while clearing summer-heat (cold); Yi Yi Ren percolating damp while fortifying the Spleen, removing bi and expelling pus (gentle, long use); Che Qian Zi benefiting water while clearing the Liver and brightening the eyes; Fang Ji moving water at the flesh-exterior and channel conduits while expelling wind-damp. First ask: draining water with supplementation (Fu Ling), pure draining (Zhu-Ze), with heat (Hua Shi-Che), with damp bi (Yi Yi Ren-Fang Ji).
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Internal water-damp with Spleen deficiency, palpitation and insomnia (benefiting within supplementing)
→
Fu Ling
Draining water and percolating damp while fortifying the Spleen and quieting the Heart — even and enduring, commonly paired in supplements (Ling Gui, Si Jun), the only water-drainer with a "supplementing" nature.
Exuberant water-damp needing focused pure draining (edema, inhibited urination)
→
Zhu Ling
Purely percolating damp, more focused than Fu Ling and without supplementing — the sovereign of Zhu Ling Tang, draining water in repletion patterns.
Water-damp with lower-burner deficiency-fire, or phlegm-rheum clouding-dizziness
→
Ze Xie
Draining water while draining Kidney fire — Ze Xie Tang treats clouding-dizziness, one of Liu Wei's three drains, benefiting with clearing.
Damp-heat strangury pain, summer-heat vexing thirst with red urine
→
Hua Shi
Sliding and benefiting the orifice-passages while clearing summer-heat — the benefiting of Ba Zheng, the sovereign of Liu Yi, the target of damp-heat.
Spleen-deficiency damp exuberance with cramping sinews, or abscess pus (gentle supplement, long use)
→
Yi Yi Ren
Percolating damp and fortifying the Spleen while removing bi and expelling pus — gentle in force and mild, bearing long use, serving both classical and later formulas.
Edema with Liver-heat red eyes, or damp-heat diarrhea
→
Che Qian Zi
Benefiting water and freeing strangury while clearing the Liver and brightening the eyes, benefiting urine to firm the stool — apt where damp-heat and Liver-heat appear together.
Water at the flesh-exterior and channel conduits (wind-water body heaviness, damp-heat bi joint swelling)
→
Fang Ji
Moving water at the flesh-exterior and channel conduits while expelling wind-damp — divided from Ling-Ze which travel the water passages, the target of damp-heat bi.
Clinical Key Points
Water-drainers mostly percolate and harm yin, so use with caution in yin-deficiency fluid depletion (Zhu Ling Tang therefore adds E Jiao to protect yin). The first trap is "draining water without distinguishing supplement and drain": Spleen-deficiency water-lodging suits Fu Ling (benefiting within supplementing), repletion congestion suits Zhu-Ze (pure draining); pure draining in a deficiency pattern makes it more deficient. The second is cold-heat — damp-heat uses Hua Shi-Che (cold), cold-damp uses Ling-Gui (warm); misjudged, cold congeals and heat blazes. Stop once it hits the mark, do not over-drain and harm fluid.
Members of This Group
Fu LingZhu LingZe XieHua ShiYi Yi RenChe Qian ZiFang Ji
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

解表药选用Exterior-Releasers: Which One

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：解表按“寒热与力度”分——辛温：麻黄发汗平喘（峻·无汗表实）、桂枝解肌调营卫（缓·有汗表虚）、荆芥防风祛风之平和（寒热皆可）、苏叶白芷散寒兼专能；辛凉：薄荷桑叶轻宣风热；先辨表证寒热、有汗无汗、兼喘兼湿兼头痛
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
风寒表实、无汗而喘、身痛脉紧
→
麻黄
发汗解表力峻、宣肺平喘——表实无汗之的；有汗、虚人、高血压慎。
风寒表虚、有汗恶风、营卫不和
→
桂枝
解肌发表、调和营卫——桂枝汤之君，与麻黄成表实表虚两极。
外感风邪（寒热皆可）、需平和祛风
→
荆芥
发散之力轻而性平——寒热两方皆可借（银翘之“制性存用”）；炒炭止血。
风邪兼湿、或需引经升清（痹痛、痛泻、固表）
→
防风
风药中之润剂：祛风胜湿兼升清引经——玉屏风、痛泻、蠲痹皆用。
风寒轻证、或气滞胸闷（凉燥、梅核气）
→
苏叶
辛温轻散兼行气宽中——散、行、和三职，与苏子叶散子降。
风寒兼阳明头面（前额眉棱痛、鼻塞）
→
白芷
散寒燥湿兼通鼻窍止痛——阳明头面之引经，专治前额之痛。
风热表证、头痛咽痛目赤、或肝郁
→
薄荷
辛凉轻散、清利头目咽喉兼疏肝——温病卫分与逍遥两用。
风热犯肺之咳、或肝热目赤
→
桑叶
疏散肺络风热兼清肝明目——桑菊之君，凉燥忌、温燥宜。
临证要点
解表第一辨寒温：风寒误辛凉则冰伏、风热误辛温则助燔——恶寒轻重与咽渴有无是分水岭。第二辨表虚实：无汗表实用麻黄、有汗表虚用桂枝，表虚误麻黄则汗漏亡阳。麻黄发汗峻，虚人、高血压、失眠者慎。辛温香燥伤阴，阴虚外感另有加减葳蕤之法。
本组成员
麻黄桂枝荆芥防风苏叶白芷薄荷桑叶
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: exterior-releasers divided by "cold-heat and strength" — acrid-warm: Ma Huang promoting sweat and calming wheeze (fierce, exterior-repletion without sweat), Gui Zhi resolving the flesh and harmonizing nutritive-defensive (gentle, exterior-deficiency with sweat), Jing Jie-Fang Feng the even wind-expellers (cold or heat alike), Su Ye-Bai Zhi dispersing cold with specialties; acrid-cool: Bo He-Sang Ye lightly diffusing wind-heat. First distinguish the exterior pattern's cold-heat, sweat or no sweat, with wheeze, damp or headache.
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Wind-cold exterior-repletion, no sweat with wheeze, body pain, tight pulse
→
Ma Huang
Fierce in promoting sweat and resolving the exterior, diffusing the Lung and calming wheeze — the target of exterior-repletion without sweat; caution with sweat, in the deficient and hypertensive.
Wind-cold exterior-deficiency, sweating with aversion to wind, nutritive-defensive disharmony
→
Gui Zhi
Resolving the flesh, effusing the exterior and harmonizing nutritive-defensive — the sovereign of Gui Zhi Tang, forming with Ma Huang the two poles of exterior-repletion and exterior-deficiency.
Wind-pathogen contraction (cold or heat), needing even wind-expulsion
→
Jing Jie
Light in dispersing force and even in nature — borrowable by both cold and heat formulas (Yin Qiao's "curbing the nature, keeping the use"); charred it stops bleeding.
Wind-pathogen with damp, or needing channel-guiding and clear-raising (bi pain, painful diarrhea, exterior-consolidation)
→
Fang Feng
The moist herb among wind herbs: expelling wind, overcoming damp, raising the clear and guiding the channel — used in Yu Ping Feng, Tong Xie, Juan Bi.
Light wind-cold, or qi-stagnation chest oppression (cool-dryness, plum-pit qi)
→
Su Ye
Acrid-warm and light-dispersing while moving qi and widening the middle — three offices of dispersing, moving and harmonizing, with Su Zi (leaf disperses, seed descends).
Wind-cold with Yang Ming head-and-face (forehead and brow-ridge pain, nasal congestion)
→
Bai Zhi
Dispersing cold, drying damp and freeing the nasal orifices while stopping pain — the channel-guide for the Yang Ming head-and-face, treating forehead pain specifically.
Wind-heat exterior pattern, headache, sore throat, red eyes, or Liver-constraint
→
Bo He
Acrid-cool and light-dispersing, clearing and benefiting the head, eyes and throat while coursing the Liver — used in both the warm-disease defensive level and Xiao Yao.
Wind-heat assailing the Lung with cough, or Liver-heat red eyes
→
Sang Ye
Coursing and dispersing wind-heat of the Lung collaterals while clearing the Liver and brightening the eyes — the sovereign of Sang Ju, shunning cool-dryness, apt for warm-dryness.
Clinical Key Points
Exterior-release first distinguishes cold and warm: wind-cold mistakenly given acrid-cool freezes and traps, wind-heat mistakenly given acrid-warm abets the blaze — the severity of aversion to cold and the presence of thirst are the watershed. Second, distinguish exterior deficiency and repletion: no sweat exterior-repletion uses Ma Huang, sweating exterior-deficiency uses Gui Zhi; exterior deficiency mistakenly given Ma Huang leaks sweat and collapses yang. Ma Huang's sweat-promoting is fierce, used with caution in the deficient, hypertensive and insomniac. Acrid-warm aromatic-drying harms yin; yin-deficiency contraction has its own Jia Jian Wei Rui method.
Members of This Group
Ma HuangGui ZhiJing JieFang FengSu YeBai ZhiBo HeSang Ye
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

补阳药选用Yang-Tonics: Which One

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：补阳按“峻缓与兼能”分——附子肉桂回阳温里之峻（急·亡阳沉寒）、鹿角胶血肉填精之厚（虚劳精亏）、淫羊藿仙茅巴戟温肾壮阳（缓·痿弱）、杜仲菟丝子平补兼固（腰膝胎元）、补骨脂益智温脾肾固下（泻遗）；先问：回阳救急（附桂）？填精（鹿胶）？壮阳（三仙）？固摄（杜菟骨智）
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
亡阳欲脱、四肢厥逆、脉微（急）
→
附子
回阳救逆之峻、走而不守——四逆之君，救急之本；有毒制用。
命门火衰、畏寒、引火归元（守）
→
肉桂
温命门、引火归元、温通血脉——与附子一走一守，右归益火。
精血亏虚之虚劳、腰膝痿软（血肉填补）
→
鹿角胶
血肉有情、峻补精血温督——左归“阳中求阴”、右归填精。
肾阳虚之阳痿宫冷、筋骨痿弱
→
淫羊藿
补肾壮阳兼祛风湿——二仙之君，痿弱痹弱通用；仙茅同类而更燥烈。
肾阳虚而需温润不燥（久服）
→
巴戟天
温肾助阳而甘润不燥——温阳药中之柔和者，可任久服。
肝肾亏之腰膝痛、胎动不安
→
杜仲
补肝肾强筋骨兼安胎——腰膝专药，与牛膝成对。
阴阳两虚、需平补固精（遗精尿频泻）
→
菟丝子
平补阴阳、固精缩尿——不燥不腻，左右归通用。
脾肾阳虚之五更泻、尿频
→
补骨脂
补命门火以暖脾土——四神之君，“补火暖土”；配益智温脾肾固下。
下元虚寒之尿频遗尿、多唾
→
益智仁
温肾缩尿、温脾摄唾——缩泉之君，固摄下元之专。
临证要点
补阳药多温燥，阴虚火旺者忌（反劫阴助火）——补阳须“阴中求阳”（右归熟地坐底）方不燥。第一陷阱是峻缓错配：亡阳急证用附桂回阳、日常虚弱用三仙杜菟缓补，缓证滥用附子则燥、急证误用缓药则不及。附子有毒必制用久煎，仙茅有毒不宜久服。湿热、实热证一概禁补阳。
本组成员
附子肉桂鹿角胶淫羊藿巴戟天杜仲菟丝子补骨脂益智仁
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: yang-supplementers divided by "fierce-gentle and secondary ability" — Fu Zi-Rou Gui the fierce restoring yang and warming the interior (urgent, yang collapse and deep cold); Lu Jiao Jiao the thick blood-flesh filling essence (taxation essence-depletion); Yin Yang Huo-Xian Mao-Ba Ji warming the Kidney and invigorating yang (gentle, atrophy-weakness); Du Zhong-Tu Si Zi even-supplementing and consolidating (lower back-knee, fetal origin); Bu Gu Zhi-Yi Zhi Ren warming Spleen-Kidney and consolidating below (diarrhea, enuresis). First ask: restore yang for emergency (Fu-Gui)? Fill essence (Lu Jiao)? Invigorate yang (the three immortals)? Consolidate (Du-Tu-Bu-Yi)?
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Yang collapse verging on desertion, reversal cold of the four limbs, faint pulse (urgent)
→
Fu Zi
The fierce restorer of yang and stemmer of reversal, traveling and not guarding — the sovereign of Si Ni, the root of acute rescue; toxic, used processed.
Ming Men fire debilitation, aversion to cold, guiding fire back to the origin (guarding)
→
Rou Gui
Warming Ming Men, guiding fire back to the origin, warming and freeing the blood vessels — one traveling, one guarding with Fu Zi, You Gui's boosting of fire.
Essence-blood depletion taxation, aching weak lower back and knees (blood-flesh filling)
→
Lu Jiao Jiao
A blood-flesh substance, fiercely supplementing essence-blood and warming the Governing vessel — Zuo Gui's "seeking yin within yang," You Gui's essence-filling.
Kidney-yang deficiency impotence and cold womb, atrophic weak sinews and bones
→
Yin Yang Huo
Supplementing the Kidney and invigorating yang while expelling wind-damp — the sovereign of Er Xian, versatile for atrophy-weakness and bi-weakness; Xian Mao of the same kind but more drying-fierce.
Kidney-yang deficiency needing warm-moist without drying (long use)
→
Ba Ji Tian
Warming the Kidney and aiding yang, sweet-moist without drying — the gentle one among yang-warmers, bearing long use.
Liver-Kidney depletion low-back-knee pain, restless fetus
→
Du Zhong
Supplementing Liver-Kidney and strengthening sinews and bones while calming the fetus — the low-back-knee specialist, paired with Niu Xi.
Yin-yang dual deficiency needing even supplementation and essence consolidation (seminal emission, urinary frequency, diarrhea)
→
Tu Si Zi
Evenly supplementing yin and yang, consolidating essence and contracting urine — neither drying nor cloying, used in both Zuo Gui and You Gui.
Spleen-Kidney-yang deficiency fifth-watch diarrhea, urinary frequency
→
Bu Gu Zhi
Supplementing Ming Men fire to warm the Spleen earth — the sovereign of Si Shen, "supplement fire to warm earth"; paired with Yi Zhi Ren to warm Spleen-Kidney and consolidate below.
Lower-origin deficiency-cold urinary frequency and enuresis, excessive drooling
→
Yi Zhi Ren
Warming the Kidney and contracting urine, warming the Spleen and restraining drool — the sovereign of Suo Quan, the specialist of consolidating the lower origin.
Clinical Key Points
Yang-supplementers are mostly warm-drying and shunned in yin-deficiency fire hyperactivity (conversely plundering yin and abetting fire) — supplementing yang must "seek yang within yin" (You Gui's Shu Di as foundation) to avoid drying. The first trap is fierce-gentle mismatch: yang collapse acute cases use Fu-Gui to restore yang, everyday weakness uses the three immortals-Du-Tu for gentle supplementation; overusing Fu Zi in a gentle case dries, misusing a gentle herb in an acute case falls short. Fu Zi is toxic and must be processed and long-boiled, Xian Mao is toxic and unfit for long use. Damp-heat and repletion-heat patterns categorically forbid yang supplementation.
Members of This Group
Fu ZiRou GuiLu Jiao JiaoYin Yang HuoBa Ji TianDu ZhongTu Si ZiBu Gu ZhiYi Zhi Ren
← 返回上页← Back首页Home

清热药选用Heat-Clearers: Which One

类药鉴别Herb Compare
鉴别主轴
鉴别主轴：清热按“热之性位”分四路——清气分（石膏知母·大热渴）、清脏火（黄连心胃/黄芩肺胆/黄柏下焦/栀子三焦·湿热火郁）、清热凉血（生地丹皮玄参·血分热）、清热解毒（金银花连翘·疮痈温毒）；先辨热在气分血分、在何脏、有无湿有无毒
临证决策链（抓手 → 选药 → 推理）
气分大热、壮热烦渴、脉洪大
→
石膏
清气分大热之君、辛寒透热——白虎之君，配知母清润。气虚寒者禁。
心火胃火、烦躁痞呕、湿热痞利
→
黄连
清心胃之火、燥湿厚肠——三黄之居中，泻心之君；大苦大寒，中病即止。
肺胆之热、湿热、胎热
→
黄芩
清上焦肺热与分野之热、燥湿安胎——三黄之居上，黄芩汤之君。
下焦湿热、阴虚火旺（骨蒸痿淋）
→
黄柏
清下焦之火兼退虚火坚阴——三黄之居下，知柏地黄之柏。
三焦火郁、心烦、湿热黄疸
→
栀子
清泻三焦、导火下行兼清郁热——栀子豉之君，火郁与湿热两用。
血分热、温毒发斑、阴虚（凉血为主）
→
地黄
生地凉血滋阴——血分热与阴伤之基，清营凉血诸方之底。
血分热兼瘀、无汗骨蒸
→
牡丹皮
凉血兼散瘀、退无汗之蒸——与地骨皮分工（丹皮退无汗蒸、地骨退有汗蒸）。
阴虚火炎、咽肿瘰疬、便秘
→
玄参
滋阴降火兼解毒软坚——增液之君、消瘰之君，滋而能行。
温病初起、疮痈热毒（透散）
→
金银花
清热解毒兼芳香透邪——甘寒不伤胃，银翘之君。
温病透表、疮痈散结、心经郁热
→
连翘
清热解毒而善散——疮家圣药、心家清散，与银花成对。
临证要点
清热药多苦寒或甘寒，易伤脾胃阳气：脾虚便溏者慎苦寒（芩连柏栀）、中病即止勿过。第一陷阱是气血不分：气分热用膏知（清透）、血分热用地丹（凉血），血分热误用气分药则不入、气分热妄用凉血药则滋腻恋邪。第二是虚实不分：实火直折（膏连）、虚火滋降（地黄知柏配滋阴），虚火误用苦寒直折则火不退而阴愈伤。苦寒败胃、甘寒滋腻，皆不可久服。
本组成员
石膏知母黄连黄芩黄柏栀子地黄牡丹皮玄参金银花连翘
Axis of Distinction
Axis of distinction: heat-clearers divided into four lines by "heat's nature and location" — clear the qi level (Shi Gao-Zhi Mu, great heat and thirst); clear visceral fire (Huang Lian for Heart-stomach / Huang Qin for Lung-Gallbladder / Huang Bai for lower burner / Zhi Zi for triple burner; damp-heat, fire-constraint); clear heat and cool blood (Sheng Di, Mu Dan Pi, Xuan Shen; blood-level heat); clear heat and resolve toxin (Jin Yin Hua, Lian Qiao; sores-abscess, warm-toxin). First distinguish whether heat is at the qi or blood level, in which viscus, with damp or with toxin.
Clinical Decision Chain (Grasp → Choose → Reason)
Qi-level great heat, vigorous heat with vexing thirst, surging large pulse
→
Shi Gao
The sovereign of clearing qi-level great heat, acrid-cold venting heat — the sovereign of Bai Hu, paired with Zhi Mu to clear and moisten. Forbidden in qi-deficiency cold.
Heart fire and stomach fire, vexation with focal fullness and retching, damp-heat focal fullness and diarrhea
→
Huang Lian
Clearing Heart-stomach fire and drying damp and thickening the gut — the middle-dwelling of the three Huang, the sovereign of Xie Xin; greatly bitter and cold, stop once it hits the mark.
Lung-Gallbladder heat, damp-heat, fetal heat
→
Huang Qin
Clearing upper-burner Lung heat and domain heat, drying damp and calming the fetus — the upper-dwelling of the three Huang, the sovereign of Huang Qin Tang.
Lower-burner damp-heat, yin-deficiency fire hyperactivity (steaming-bone, atrophy, strangury)
→
Huang Bo
Clearing lower-burner fire while abating deficiency-fire and firming yin — the lower-dwelling of the three Huang, the Huang Bai of Zhi Bai Di Huang.
Triple-burner fire-constraint, vexation, damp-heat jaundice
→
Zhi Zi
Clearing and draining the triple burner, conducting fire downward while clearing constrained heat — the sovereign of Zhi Zi Chi, used in both fire-constraint and damp-heat.
Blood-level heat, warm-toxin maculae, yin deficiency (cooling blood predominant)
→
Di Huang
Sheng Di cooling blood and enriching yin — the foundation of blood-level heat and yin damage, the base of the nutritive-clearing blood-cooling formulas.
Blood-level heat with stasis, sweatless steaming-bone
→
Mu Dan Pi
Cooling blood while dispersing stasis, abating sweatless steaming — divided from Di Gu Pi (Mu Dan Pi abates sweatless steaming, Di Gu abates sweating steaming).
Yin-deficiency fire flaming, throat swelling and scrofula, constipation
→
Xuan Shen
Enriching yin and downbearing fire while resolving toxin and softening hardness — the sovereign of Zeng Ye, the sovereign of Xiao Luo, enriching yet able to move.
Early warm disease, sore-abscess heat-toxin (venting-dispersing)
→
Jin Yin Hua
Clearing heat and resolving toxin while aromatically venting the pathogen — sweet-cold without harming the stomach, the sovereign of Yin Qiao.
Warm disease venting the exterior, sore-abscess bind-dissipating, Heart-channel constrained heat
→
Lian Yao
Clearing heat and resolving toxin while good at dispersing — the sage-herb of sores, the Heart-family clearing-disperser, paired with Jin Yin Hua.
Clinical Key Points
Heat-clearers are mostly bitter-cold or sweet-cold, easily harming Spleen-Stomach yang qi: use bitter-cold (Qin-Lian-Bai-Zhi) with caution in Spleen-deficiency loose stool, stop once it hits the mark. The first trap is not distinguishing qi and blood: qi-level heat uses Shi Gao-Zhi Mu (clear-venting), blood-level heat uses Sheng Di-Mu Dan Pi (cooling blood); blood-level heat mistakenly given qi-level herbs does not enter, qi-level heat wrongly given blood-cooling herbs cloys and lodges the pathogen. The second is not distinguishing repletion and deficiency: repletion fire cut directly (Shi Gao-Huang Lian), deficiency fire enriched and downborne (Di Huang, Zhi-Bai with yin-enrichment); deficiency fire mistakenly cut by bitter-cold leaves the fire unabated and the yin more harmed. Bitter-cold spoils the stomach, sweet-cold cloys — neither for long use.
Members of This Group
Shi GaoZhi MuHuang LianHuang QinHuang BoZhi ZiDi HuangMu Dan PiXuan ShenJin Yin HuaLian Yao
